items.title: Canada and Manitoba join forces to give new momentum to Francophone immigration
items.bodyEn:
From: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

News release

Canada is committed to supporting Francophone immigration and is focused on attracting and retaining the world’s best talent. As Francophone minority communities face significant demographic and economic challenges, the governments of Canada and Manitoba are joining forces to strengthen official language minority communities by addressing their labour needs and fostering their economic and linguistic vitality.

July 6, 2026—Winnipeg – Canada is committed to supporting Francophone immigration and is focused on attracting and retaining the world’s best talent. As Francophone minority communities face significant demographic and economic challenges, the governments of Canada and Manitoba are joining forces to strengthen official language minority communities by addressing their labour needs and fostering their economic and linguistic vitality.

Today, the Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship, announced an investment of approximately $1.3 million to support a new project funded through the Francophone Immigration Support Program. The aim of this project is to grow Manitoba’s Francophone population through innovative promotional initiatives and digital solutions, targeted research and increased support for recognition of skills. These measures streamline the immigration process for Francophone and bilingual candidates who meet labour market needs.

The project will be implemented in partnership with the Economic Development Council for Manitoba’s Bilingual Municipalities, the province’s Francophone economic development organization.

Consult the backgrounder to learn more about the project.

The Minister also announced that the Francophone Minority Communities Student Pilot (FMCSP) will be extended for an additional year, through August 2027. Thanks to this extension, Canada will continue to attract French-speaking international students who contribute to the vitality of Francophone minority communities and the growth of the Canadian economy, and encourage them to remain in the country.

Together, this investment, the extension of the pilot program and the ongoing collaboration between the governments of Canada and Manitoba will help grow Manitoba’s economy by addressing labour needs through Francophone immigration, while strengthening the vitality and sustainability of Francophone minority communities.

Quotes

“This announcement, made in partnership with the Government of Manitoba, marks an important step in supporting Francophone immigration. It strengthens the economic and demographic growth of Francophone minority communities and addresses labour market needs. It is a concrete step toward ensuring the vitality and sustainability of Franco-Manitoban communities and a new step forward in achieving the Government of Canada’s ambitious goal for Francophone immigration.”

– The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship

“Francophone minority communities across the country are an integral part of Canada’s national identity. Here in Manitoba, the federal government is proud to support them by welcoming Francophone students and newcomers, who make such a significant contribution to our culture and to our local, regional and national economies.”

– Ginette Lavack, Parliamentary Secretary to the Minister of Indigenous Services, and Member of Parliament for Saint-Boniface–Saint-Vital, Manitoba

Quick facts

• The Government of Canada is investing $25 million over 5 years in the Centre for Innovation in Francophone Immigration as part of the Action Plan for Official Languages 2023–2028. To date, 20 projects have received funding, totalling approximately $15.7 million to increase the recruitment of Francophone and bilingual talent and to support Francophone minority communities. Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada’s ongoing commitment to Francophone immigration is reflected in several successes over the past year, notably achieving an 8.9% admission rate for French­speaking permanent residents outside Quebec in 2025. The FMCSP aims to support Francophone minority communities while strengthening the diversity of the International Student Program (ISP). This pilot program facilitates access to the ISP for Francophone students from regions of the world with historically high study permit refusal rates. Individuals who apply to a participating designated learning institution may be selected through the pilot, receive settlement and integration services during their studies, and apply for permanent residence after graduating from an eligible program. Immediate family members of FMCSP participants may accompany them to Canada. To date, 515 Francophone students and 150 accompanying family members have arrived in Canada under the FMCSP. The first FMCSP participants could graduate and apply for permanent residence as early as 2027.

Related products

• Projects aimed at supporting Francophone minority communities

Associated links

• Centre for Innovation in Francophone Immigration

• Mandate Letter from the Prime Minister of Canada

• Action Plan for Official Languages 2023–2028: Protection-Promotion-Collaboration

• Immigrate using Express Entry

• Immigrate to a Francophone community outside Quebec

• Francophone Minority Communities Student Pilot

Page details
items.bodyZh:
来源：加拿大移民、难民和公民身份部

新闻稿

加拿大致力于支持法语移民，专注于吸引和保留世界顶尖人才。随着法语少数族裔社区面临重大人口和经济挑战，加拿大和曼尼托巴省政府携手合作，通过解决其劳动力需求并促进其经济和语言活力，来加强官方语言少数族裔社区。

2026年7月6日——温尼伯 — 加拿大致力于支持法语移民，专注于吸引和保留世界顶尖人才。随着法语少数族裔社区面临重大人口和经济挑战，加拿大和曼尼托巴省政府携手合作，通过解决其劳动力需求并促进其经济和语言活力，来加强官方语言少数族裔社区。

今日，移民、难民和公民身份部部长莱娜·梅特莱格·迪亚布（Lena Metlege Diab）宣布投资约130万加元，以支持一个通过《法语移民支持项目》（Francophone Immigration Support Program）资助的新项目。该项目的目标是通过创新的推广举措和数字解决方案、有针对性的研究以及加强技能认可支持，促进曼尼托巴省法语人口的增长。这些措施简化了符合劳动力市场需求的法语和双语候选人的移民流程。

该项目将与《曼尼托巴省双语市镇经济发展委员会》（Economic Development Council for Manitoba's Bilingual Municipalities）——该省的法语经济发展组织——合作实施。

请查阅背景资料了解更多有关该项目的信息。

部长还宣布，《法语少数族裔社区学生试点项目》（Francophone Minority Communities Student Pilot, FMCSP）将额外延期一年，至2027年8月。得益于这一延期，加拿大将继续吸引法语国际学生，他们为法语少数族裔社区的活力和加拿大经济增长做出贡献，并鼓励他们留在国内。

综合来看，这笔投资、试点项目的延期以及加拿大与曼尼托巴省政府的持续合作，将通过法语移民解决劳动力需求，帮助促进曼尼托巴省经济增长，同时加强法语少数族裔社区的活力和可持续性。

引述内容

"这一由曼尼托巴省政府联合宣布的公告标志着支持法语移民的重要一步。它强化了法语少数族裔社区的经济和人口增长，并解决了劳动力市场需求。这是确保弗朗哥-曼尼托巴社区活力和可持续性的具体措施，也是加拿大政府实现法语移民宏大目标的新进展。"

——移民、难民和公民身份部部长敬礼莱娜·梅特莱格·迪亚布

"全国各地的法语少数族裔社区是加拿大国家认同的重要组成部分。在曼尼托巴省这里，联邦政府荣幸能通过欢迎法语学生和新移民来支持这些社区，他们对我们的文化以及当地、区域和全国经济做出了重大贡献。"

——吉内特·拉瓦克（Ginette Lavack），原住民服务部部长议会秘书，曼尼托巴省圣博尼法斯-圣维塔尔选区议员

速览信息

• 加拿大政府在《2023–2028年官方语言行动计划》框架下，向《法语移民创新中心》（Centre for Innovation in Francophone Immigration）投资2500万加元（为期5年）。迄今为止，已有20个项目获得资金支持，总额约为1570万加元，用于增加法语和双语人才的招聘，以及支持法语少数族裔社区。加拿大移民、难民和公民身份部对法语移民的持续承诺在过去一年取得了多项成就，特别是在2025年实现魁北克以外地区法语永久居民的录取率达8.9%。《法语少数族裔社区学生试点项目》旨在支持法语少数族裔社区，同时加强《国际学生项目》（International Student Program, ISP）的多样性。该试点项目为来自历来学习许可拒签率较高地区的法语学生提供了《国际学生项目》的便捷途径。申请参与指定学习机构的个人可能通过该试点被选中，在学习期间接受安置和融合服务，并在从符合条件的项目毕业后申请永久居留。《法语少数族裔社区学生试点项目》参与者的直系亲属可陪同其赴加拿大。迄今为止，已有515名法语学生和150名陪同的家庭成员根据《法语少数族裔社区学生试点项目》抵达加拿大。首批《法语少数族裔社区学生试点项目》参与者最早可在2027年毕业并申请永久居留。

items.title: Expression of Interest Draw #274
items.bodyEn:
Skilled Worker Stream

Profiles submitted under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba pathway or the Skilled Worker Overseas pathway that declared being directly invited by the MPNP under a strategic recruitment initiative.

• Number of Letters of Advice to Apply issued: 77

The following numbers of Letters of Advice to Apply were issued to candidates declaring receipt of an Invitation to Apply (ITA) under the strategic recruitment initiatives listed below:

• Employer Services: 36

• Francophone Community: 8

• Regional Communities: 2

• Temporary Public Policy to Facilitate Work Permits for Prospective Provincial Nominee Program Candidates (TPP): 31

Of the 77 Letters of Advice to Apply issued in this draw, 15 were issued to candidates who declared a valid Express Entry profile number and job seeker validation code.

Notes

• Expressions of Interest from Skilled Worker Stream candidates have only been considered if they were directly invited by the MPNP under a strategic recruitment initiative or through the Temporary Public Policy to Facilitate Work Permits for Prospective Provincial Nominee Program Candidates.

• If you have submitted an EOI profile which meets the above listed selection criteria but you have not received a Letter of Advice to Apply, this could be because you have indicated that you have taken an approved third-party language test to demonstrate your language ability in English/French, but have not provided a valid test number in your EOI or your language test is no longer valid; or you have indicated that you have received an Invitation to Apply to the MPNP under a strategic recruitment initiative, but did not provide a valid invitation number.

• Please note that for each question in the EOI, help text is available, providing detailed information on the information you should enter. If one of these scenarios applies to you, you may wish to update your EOI with the correct information in order to be considered for the next draw from the EOI pool.
bodyZh: 技能工人流(Skilled Worker Stream)
在曼尼托巴省技能工人途径(Skilled Worker in Manitoba pathway)或海外技能工人途径(Skilled Worker Overseas pathway)下提交的意向书档案,且申请人声明已被MPNP在战略招聘计划下直接邀请。
• 发放的申请建议函数量:77份
以下是根据申请人声称收到的MPNP战略招聘计划下申请邀请(ITA)而发放申请建议函的申请人数量明细:

• 雇主服务(Employer Services):36份
• 法语社区(Francophone Community):8份
• 地区社区(Regional Communities):2份
• 便利省提名计划候选人工作许可的临时公共政策(TPP — Temporary Public Policy to Facilitate Work Permits for Prospective Provincial Nominee Program Candidates):31份
在本轮抽签中发放的77份申请建议函中,有15份发放给了声明拥有有效快速通道档案编号和求职者验证码的申请人。
备注
• 只有在以下情况下才会考虑技能工人流申请人提交的意向书:申请人被MPNP在战略招聘计划下直接邀请,或通过便利省提名计划候选人工作许可的临时公共政策获得邀请。
• 如果您提交的意向书档案符合上述选择标准,但您未收到申请建议函,可能原因是:您声明已参加获批的第三方语言考试以证明英语/法语能力,但未在意向书中提供有效的考试编号,或您的语言考试成绩已过期;或您声明已收到MPNP战略招聘计划下的申请邀请,但未提供有效的邀请编号。
• 请注意,意向书中的每个问题都配有帮助文本,提供有关您应输入信息的详细信息。如果以上任何情况适用于您,您可能希望使用正确信息更新您的意向书档案,以便在下一次意向书池抽签中被考虑。
items.summaryZh: 曼尼托巴省提名计划(MPNP)在第274次意向书抽签中向技能工人流(Skilled Worker Stream)申请人发放了77份申请建议函(LOA),这些申请人都是通过战略招聘计划或临时工作许可政策直接获得邀请的。其中15份LOA申请人同时拥有有效的快速通道档案信息。

items.title: Statement by Minister Metlege Diab on Canada Day
items.bodyEn:
From: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

Statement

“Each year on Canada Day, I reflect on how fortunate we are to call this country home—a place where diversity is celebrated, our values unite us, and our collective identity comes to life through our official languages and the experiences we share. The occasion is a time to celebrate what brings us together, take pride in our communities and look to the future with confidence as we build an even better Canada for the generations that will follow us."

Ottawa, July 1, 2026 —The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship, issued the following statement on Canada Day:

“Each year on Canada Day , I reflect on how fortunate we are to call this country home—a place where diversity is celebrated, our values unite us, and our collective identity comes to life through our official languages and the experiences we share. The occasion is a time to celebrate what brings us together, take pride in our communities and look to the future with confidence as we build an even better Canada for the generations that will follow us.

“As part of this year’s festivities, I am honoured to be attending citizenship ceremonies in Canada’s capital. These ceremonies are part of a large celebration taking place in every corner of our country where newcomers are taking the oath of citizenship. This is an emotional and meaningful moment as they officially join the Canadian family. These ceremonies happen year-round across Canada and reflect the hope and determination of those who choose to build their lives here. If you ever have the chance to attend one , I highly recommend it; it is a powerful experience.

“Generations of newcomers and their descendants have contributed to Canada’s success. Today, we are a proud, welcoming, dynamic country precisely because we know how to find common cause and get big things done. Whether your family has been here for generations or you are taking the oath of citizenship for the first time today, you are part of Canada’s story—one of unity, opportunity and progress for all Canadians.

“On this Canada Day, I also want to recognize the First Nations, Inuit and Métis Peoples who have lived on this land since time immemorial and continue to shape Canada’s development. Their histories, cultures, traditions and contributions are essential to who we are, and all Canadians should strive to learn more about Indigenous Peoples while walking the path of reconciliation.

“Today, on Canada Day, let’s celebrate our nation’s best days and our bright future. Happy Canada Day!

“For more information about Canada Day celebrations in your area, visit the Canadian Heritage website .”

For further information (media only), please contact:

Taous Ait Senior Communications Advisor and Press Secretary Office of the Minister Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Taous.Ait@cic.gc.ca

Media Relations People and Communications Sector Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada media@cic.gc.ca

Page details
items.bodyZh:
来源：加拿大移民、难民和公民部

声明

"每年在加拿大国庆日，我都会反思我们能够将这个国家称为家园是多么幸运——在这里，多样性得到庆祝，我们的价值观将我们团结在一起，我们的集体身份通过我们的官方语言和我们共享的经历而得以实现。这个时刻是庆祝团结我们的东西、为我们的社区感到骄傲，并自信地展望未来，为随后的世代建设一个更美好的加拿大的时间。"

2026年7月1日，渥太华——加拿大移民、难民和公民部部长荣誉阁下Lena Metlege Diab在加拿大国庆日发表了以下声明：

"每年在加拿大国庆日，我都会反思我们能够将这个国家称为家园是多么幸运——在这里，多样性得到庆祝，我们的价值观将我们团结在一起，我们的集体身份通过我们的官方语言和我们共享的经历而得以实现。这个时刻是庆祝团结我们的东西、为我们的社区感到骄傲，并自信地展望未来，为随后的世代建设一个更美好的加拿大的时间。

"作为今年庆祝活动的一部分，我荣幸能够参加在加拿大首都举办的入籍仪式。这些仪式是在我们国家各个角落进行的大型庆祝活动的一部分，新移民们正在宣誓入籍。这是一个富有感情和意义的时刻，因为他们正式加入加拿大大家庭。这些仪式全年在加拿大各地举行，反映了选择在这里建立生活的人们的希望和决心。如果你有机会参加其中一场，我强烈推荐；这是一个强大而有力的体验。

"几代新移民及其后代对加拿大的成功做出了贡献。今天，我们是一个骄傲、热情、充满活力的国家，正是因为我们知道如何找到共同点并完成大事。无论你的家族在这里已经生活了多代，还是你今天第一次宣誓入籍，你都是加拿大故事的一部分——这是一个关于团结、机遇和所有加拿大人进步的故事。

"在这个加拿大国庆日，我也想表彰自古以来就生活在这片土地上的第一民族、因纽特人和梅蒂斯人，他们继续塑造加拿大的发展。他们的历史、文化、传统和贡献对于我们的身份至关重要，所有加拿大人都应该努力更多地了解土著民族，同时走上和解的道路。

"今天，在加拿大国庆日，让我们庆祝我们国家最美好的日子和光明的未来。加拿大国庆日快乐！

"欲了解更多关于你所在地区加拿大国庆日庆祝活动的信息，请访问加拿大遗产网站。"

如需进一步信息（仅限媒体），请联系：

Taous Ait 高级传播顾问及新闻秘书 移民、难民和公民部部长办公室 加拿大移民、难民和公民部 Taous.Ait@cic.gc.ca

媒体关系 人力和传播部门 加拿大移民、难民和公民部 media@cic.gc.ca

页面详情
items.summaryZh: 加拿大移民部长Lena Metlege Diab在加拿大国庆日(7月1日)发表声明,强调了多样性、共同价值观和新移民融入的重要性。她将出席首都的入籍仪式,并呼吁全国庆祝这一时刻,同时也向首先民族、因纽特人和梅蒂斯人致敬。

items.title: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program Redesign Phase 1
items.bodyEn:
The Ministry of Labour, Immigration, Training and Skills Development has made changes to Ontario Regulation 422/17 under the Ontario Immigration Act, 2015 ( OIA ). These amendments came into force on June 25, 2026.

The changes include removing the existing 8 streams and introducing the new Ontario Workforce Priority stream. The Ontario Workforce Priority stream Expression of Interest ( EOI ) system is anticipated to open later in the summer.

This represents the first of a 2-phase program redesign that will:

• streamline pathways to permanent residence for individuals with arranged employment in Ontario

• help employers retain proven talent in hard-to-fill roles

• introduce new program requirements, including elevated language and education benchmarks, to enhance the calibre of nominees supported by the province and strengthen program integrity

• improve access for employers in rural and northern communities by creating more flexible business revenue thresholds

These changes reflect feedback gathered through the Ontario Regulatory Registry and additional consultations.

The Ontario Workforce Priority stream will have pathways for individuals with job offers in all National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) Training, Education, Experience and Responsibilities ( TEERs ), as well as for self-employed physicians. Details can be found below.

Regulatory amendments also introduce changes to how notices for contraventions are issued. This is part of the Ministry’s ongoing efforts to strengthen program integrity through enhanced screening, inspections and compliance and enforcement activities against bad actors.

Candidates who have registered an Expression of Interest ( EOI ) or submitted an application

The Expression of Interest ( EOI ) system is now closed to new EOI s. No further invitations will be issued under the former program streams. EOI s and job offers registered under the former streams that have not resulted in an invitation to apply will be automatically withdrawn over the coming weeks while the EOI and application platforms are updated. Affected registrants, employers and representatives will receive a notice directly.

Candidates and employers are invited to familiarize themselves with the new Ontario Workforce Priority stream and its requirements. Eligible candidates may register a new EOI under the Ontario Workforce Priority Stream once the EOI system reopens. Employers who have previously registered in the employer portal will not need to register again when the EOI system reopens. Once the Employer Portal reopens they will need to submit a new job offer and a new application for an approval of an employment position (re-register a new job offer) to initiate a new EOI for the applicant under the new program stream.

Applications submitted following an invitation under a former stream will continue to be assessed against the eligibility requirements that were in effect when the application was submitted.

Ontario Workforce Priority Stream Overview

TEER 0-3 pathway

This pathway targets skilled internationally trained workers in all TEER 0, 1, 2, or 3 occupations with a full-time and permanent job offer in Ontario. Minimum eligibility requirements include:

• work experience : applicants must demonstrate one of the following: 6 months consecutive in the last 12 months in the job offer position with the job offer employer for recent Ontario graduates, 3 months consecutive in the last 12 months in the job offer position with the job offer employer 2 years cumulative in the last 5 years in the NOC occupation licenced applicants are exempt from this requirement.

• language requirement : Canadian Language Benchmark ( CLB ) 6, CLB 5 for certain occupations

• minimum education : post-secondary degree or diploma

Some occupations may have alternate criteria, consult the regulations for detailed requirements.

Self-employed physicians

Self-employed physicians may qualify for the Ontario Workforce Priority stream without a job offer. To qualify, candidates must demonstrate all of the following requirements:

• be a member in good standing with the College of Physicians and Surgeons of Ontario

• hold a valid certificate of registration in an eligible class: independent, academic or provisional

• be eligible to bill through the Ontario Health Insurance Plan ( OHIP ).

TEER 4-5 pathway

This pathway is open to workers in all TEER 4-5 occupations with a full-time and permanent job offer in Ontario. Minimum eligibility requirements include:

• work experience : 9 months cumulative experience in the last 2 years in the job offer position with the job offer employer

• language : CLB 4

• minimum education : Canadian secondary school diploma or equivalent

For all Ontario Workforce Priority Stream pathways, lower gross annual revenue requirements will apply to employers located in rural communities. For the purposes of the program, a rural community is a community located in a census division with a population of less than 150,000.

Program integrity changes

The OINP has reduced the response time for individuals who have been issued a Notice of Intent to Issue an Administrative Monetary Penalty ( AMP ) or Ban order from 60 to 30 days. Regulatory amendments also allow notices of contravention to be sent by email, mail, or in person, and to deem them delivered rather than requiring proof of receipt.

These changes align the response time and approach for AMP and Ban notices with those of other OINP processes, such as Notices of Intent to Refuse and Notices of Intent to Cancel a Nomination and enable faster compliance actions.

Closed streams

As part of the redesign of the OINP , the following streams have been closed:

• Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker

• Employer Job Offer: In-Demand Skills

• Employer Job Offer: International Student

• Master’s Graduate

• PhD Graduate

• Express Entry Human Capital Priorities

• Express Entry French-speaking Skilled Worker

• Express Entry Skilled Trades

Effective date for the regulatory changes

These changes came into effect today. Regulatory changes are made under amending regulation 204-26 which can be found on Ontario’s e-Laws.

Future updates

Please continue to monitor this page for the latest information.
items.bodyZh:
安大略劳动力、移民、培训和技能发展部对《2015年安大略移民法》(Ontario Immigration Act, 2015, OIA)下的《安大略条例422/17》进行了修订。这些修订于2026年6月25日生效。

修订内容包括移除现有的8个申请流,并推出新的安大略劳动力优先流(Ontario Workforce Priority stream)。安大略劳动力优先流的意向表达(EOI)系统预计将在夏季晚期开放。

这代表为期2阶段的计划改革的第一阶段,该改革将:

• 为在安大略有安排就业的个人简化永久居民身份途径

• 帮助雇主在难以招聘的职位上留住经验证明的人才

• 引入新的计划要求,包括提高语言和教育基准,以提升该省提名人员的素质并加强计划完整性

• 通过创建更灵活的商业收入门槛,改善农村和北部社区雇主的申请机会

这些变化反映了通过安大略监管登记处(Ontario Regulatory Registry)收集的反馈和进一步咨询所得意见。

安大略劳动力优先流将为所有国家职业分类(National Occupational Classification, NOC)培训、教育、经验和责任级别(Training, Education, Experience and Responsibilities, TEERs)中有工作机会的个人以及自雇执业医生提供途径。详细信息见下文。

监管修订还对违反规定通知的发放方式进行了修改。这是该部持续努力加强计划完整性的一部分,通过加强筛选、检查和针对不当行为人的合规执法活动来实现。

已注册意向表达(EOI)或提交申请的候选人

意向表达(EOI)系统现已关闭,不再接受新的EOI。原申请流下不会再发放任何邀请。在原申请流下注册但尚未获得申请邀请的EOI和工作机会将在未来数周内随着EOI和申请平台的更新而自动撤销。受影响的申请人、雇主和代理人将直接收到通知。

邀请候选人和雇主熟悉新的安大略劳动力优先流及其要求。符合条件的候选人可在EOI系统重新开放后在安大略劳动力优先流下注册新的EOI。之前在雇主门户网站注册过的雇主在EOI系统重新开放时无需重新注册。一旦雇主门户网站重新开放,他们需要提交新的工作机会和新的工作位置批准申请(重新注册新的工作机会)以便为申请人在新申请流下启动新的EOI。

在原申请流邀请后提交的申请将继续按照提交申请时生效的资格要求进行评估。

安大略劳动力优先流概览

TEER 0-3途径

该途径针对在所有TEER 0、1、2或3职业中拥有安大略全职永久工作机会的国际培训的技术工人。最低资格要求包括:

• 工作经验:申请人必须证明以下条件之一:对于安大略近期毕业生,在过去12个月内与工作机会雇主在工作机会职位上连续工作6个月;在过去12个月内与工作机会雇主在工作机会职位上连续工作3个月;在过去5年内在NOC职业中累计工作2年;持证申请人豁免此要求。

• 语言要求:加拿大语言基准(Canadian Language Benchmark, CLB)6级,某些职业为CLB 5级

• 最低教育:中学后学位或文凭

某些职业可能有替代标准,请查阅监管文件了解详细要求。

自雇执业医生

自雇执业医生可不需要工作机会而符合安大略劳动力优先流的条件。申请人必须证明满足以下所有要求:

• 与安大略医学与外科学会(College of Physicians and Surgeons of Ontario)良好信誉地保持会员资格

• 持有符合条件类别的有效执业证书:独立执业、学术或临时类别

• 有资格通过安大略健康保险计划(Ontario Health Insurance Plan, OHIP)收费。

TEER 4-5途径

该途径向所有TEER 4-5职业中拥有安大略全职永久工作机会的工人开放。最低资格要求包括:

• 工作经验:在过去2年内与工作机会雇主在工作机会职位上累计工作9个月

• 语言:CLB 4级

• 最低教育:加拿大中学文凭或同等学历

对于所有安大略劳动力优先流途径,位于农村社区的雇主将适用更低的年总收入要求。就本计划而言,农村社区是指普查区人口少于150,000的社区。

计划完整性变化

OINP将已获颁行政货币处罚(Administrative Monetary Penalty, AMP)或禁令意向通知(Notice of Intent to Issue an Administrative Monetary Penalty or Ban order)的个人的回应期从60天减少至30天。监管修订还允许违反规定通知通过电子邮件、邮件或亲自送达,并视为已送达,而无需证明收据。

这些变化使AMP和禁令通知的回应期和方式与OINP的其他流程(如拒绝意向通知和取消提名意向通知)相一致,并加快合规行动。

已关闭的申请流

作为OINP改革的一部分,以下申请流已关闭:

• 雇主工作机会:外国工人(Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker)

• 雇主工作机会:紧缺技能(Employer Job Offer: In-Demand Skills)

• 雇主工作机会:国际学生(Employer Job Offer: International Student)

• 硕士毕业生(Master's Graduate)

• 博士毕业生(PhD Graduate)

• 快速通道人力资本优先(Express Entry Human Capital Priorities)

• 快速通道法语技术工人(Express Entry French-speaking Skilled Worker)

• 快速通道技能行业(Express Entry Skilled Trades)

监管变化的生效日期

这些变化自今日起生效。监管变化通过修订条例204-26进行,可在安大略电子法律网站(Ontario's e-Laws)上查阅。

未来更新

请继续关注此页面以获取最新信息。
items.summaryZh: 安大略省移民提名计划(OINP)进行第一阶段改革，自2026年6月25日起生效。新改革撤销原有8个申请流，推出新的"安大略劳动力优先"流(Ontario Workforce Priority stream),该流的意向表达(EOI)系统预计夏季晚期开放。改革旨在为有安大略就业安排的申请人简化永久居民身份途径,同时提高语言和教育要求以增强申请人质量和计划完整性。

items.title: B.C. Newcomers’ Guide now available in six new languages
items.bodyEn: B.C. Newcomers’ Guide now available in six new languages The Province is expanding access to the 2025 B.C. Newcomers’ Guide with the release of six language editions, helping people arriving in British Columbia access important information more easily. Beginning June 2026, print and PDF versions of the guide will be available in French, Simplified Chinese, Punjabi, Spanish, Arabic and Tagalog, in addition to English. Newcomers can also download the guide online or order printed copies through WelcomeBC.ca . To further support accessibility, online translation is also available in more than 200 languages. The B.C. Newcomers’ Guide provides key information about settling in the province, including topics such as health care, housing, education, employment, driver’s licences and other services. Information Bulletin
items.bodyZh:
不列颠哥伦比亚省扩展了《2025年不列颠哥伦比亚新移民指南》的语言版本，从2026年6月起将提供法语、简体中文、旁遮普语、西班牙语、阿拉伯语和他加禄语等六种新语言的印刷版和PDF版本，以帮助到达BC省的人们更容易地获取重要信息。

该省正在扩大《2025年BC新移民指南》(B.C. Newcomers' Guide)的使用范围，发布六个语言版本，帮助抵达不列颠哥伦比亚省的人们更轻松地获取重要信息。从2026年6月开始，除英文外，该指南的印刷版和PDF版本还将提供法语、简体中文、旁遮普语、西班牙语、阿拉伯语和他加禄语版本。新移民还可以通过WelcomeBC.ca网站在线下载该指南或订购印刷版本。为进一步支持无障碍访问，在线翻译还支持200多种语言。

《BC新移民指南》提供了关于在该省定居的关键信息，包括医疗保健、住房、教育、就业、驾驶执照和其他服务等主题。

信息公告

items.title: Federal–Provincial–Territorial Immigration Ministers meet to discuss sustainable immigration
items.bodyEn:
From: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

News release

The Forum of Ministers Responsible for Immigration (FMRI) met today to advance collaboration on the 2027–2029 Immigration Levels Plan and discuss federal–provincial–territorial (FPT) priorities. They discussed returning immigration to sustainable levels while considering regional, rural and northern contexts, addressing labour shortages, reinforcing key economic sectors, and supporting communities across the country. Ministers also discussed the role of Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) in advancing these considerations and the need for stable and adequate PNP allocations.

June 23, 2026—Ottawa— The Forum of Ministers Responsible for Immigration (FMRI) met today to advance collaboration on the 2027–2029 Immigration Levels Plan and discuss federal–provincial–territorial (FPT) priorities. They discussed returning immigration to sustainable levels while considering regional, rural and northern contexts, addressing labour shortages, reinforcing key economic sectors, and supporting communities across the country. Ministers also discussed the role of Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) in advancing these considerations and the need for stable and adequate PNP allocations.

While ministers held forward-looking discussions, they also reviewed the implementation of recent reforms, including measures to support sustainable immigration and strengthen program integrity. Ministers agreed that meaningful consultation, careful planning, and effective management across both permanent and temporary immigration pathways is essential to restoring balance to Canada’s immigration system and alignment with economic objectives. Ministers discussed the federal commitment to stabilize permanent resident admissions to less than 1% of the total population beyond 2027 and to reduce the temporary population to less than 5% of the total population by the end of 2027, agreeing on the need for continued progress in returning immigration to sustainable levels.

Ministers underscored that immigration is a shared jurisdiction and that provinces and territories need to be engaged as substantive partners in the development of immigration levels and programs to enhance responsiveness to distinct labour market and demographic needs across Canada. Ministers agreed on the importance of strong collaboration between FPT partners, as well as with strategic stakeholders, while ensuring successful integration of newcomers in key labour sectors at the national and regional levels. They emphasized that attracting and retaining the world’s best talent with the skills and experience needed to grow the economy at the national and regional levels is key to achieving Canada’s economic objectives. PT ministers requested further information on the process to set PNP targets and allocations to facilitate more strategic policy and program planning.

Ministers discussed improving economic immigration programs, including modernizing processes, streamlining workflows, and reducing duplication of selection of candidates. They discussed ways to improve the Express Entry system to better address regional labour market needs and newcomer outcomes. PT ministers underlined that the PNP and other regional economic programs such as the Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP), are the most effective tools available to address regional labour shortages in key sectors, enabling provinces and territories to directly select the skilled workers that align with their economic priorities. PT ministers underscored the importance of increasing the PNP and AIP allocations to ensure provinces and territories have greater control over economic immigration.

Ministers highlighted the need for greater complementarity across immigration programs and initiatives to facilitate the transition of workers and international students already in Canada from temporary to permanent residence, while ensuring sustainable levels of new arrivals. They reinforced that these coordination approaches need to support immigrant integration in a holistic manner through aligned settlement services and programs. Ministers emphasized the role PTs play in the integration of newcomers. PT ministers urged the federal government to reinstate adequate and responsive levels of funding, in areas such as targeted language training, to support ongoing settlement needs for newcomers and help them succeed.

Ministers discussed the importance of attracting and recruiting international students to support Canada’s economy. PT ministers agreed to further collaboration to shape the Post-Graduation Work Permit Program to better align with regional labour market needs.

Ministers acknowledged prior efforts and the progress made to reduce barriers to foreign credential recognition (FCR), including occupation-specific reforms (particularly in health care), FCR system reforms, and the introduction of fair registration practices legislation in most jurisdictions. They directed officials to work expeditiously and collaboratively through the newly created Forum of Labour Market Ministers - FMRI Joint Task Force on a coordinated approach to tackle challenges and barriers across the entire immigration continuum, from pre-arrival through to employment.

Ministers expressed support for Francophone immigration as a priority, recognizing the role it can play in strengthening Francophone minority communities outside Quebec, including supporting their integration into our communities. They acknowledged this work is being undertaken to help achieve the Government of Canada’s target of 12% Francophone permanent resident admissions by 2029. They discussed progress toward the development of a renewed Federal/Provincial/Territorial Action Plan for Increasing Francophone Immigration Outside of Quebec and welcomed collaboration and complementarity with the Ministers’ Council on the Canadian Francophonie in advancing this work.

Together, ministers committed to working in close, meaningful and timely collaboration on all manner of immigration policies and programs to ensure the best possible outcomes for Canada and Canadians.

Note : As requested by the Government of Quebec, the Quebec minister responsible for immigration holds observer status at the federal and provincial-territorial ministers’ table. Under the Canada-Québec Accord, Quebec assumes sole responsibility for establishing immigration levels and for the selection, francization and integration of immigrants. In areas under its responsibility, Quebec develops its policies and programs, and legislates, regulates and sets its own standards. Quebec is not a party to the discussions and is not bound by the decisions made regarding pan-Canadian initiatives related to FCR.

About the FMRI: The FMRI is an FPT decision‑making body with the goal of supporting a flexible, timely and effective immigration system for Canada. The FMRI’s mission is to advance joint immigration priorities and to enhance Canada’s immigration policies and programs.

Quotes

“Canadians expect their governments to manage migration responsibly, balancing sustainability and system integrity with efforts to attract the best talent in the world. Alongside our partners, we are doing that work now. By restoring confidence and control to our system, we will maximize the social and economic benefits that newcomers can bring to communities across Canada. I look forward to collaborating further on our shared priorities in the months and years ahead.”

– The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship

“ Provinces and territories play a critical role in shaping immigration pathways that reflect regional labour market needs, support economic growth, and help communities of all sizes thrive. I am encouraged by the progress we’ve made together on many issues including advancing collaboration on immigration levels, strengthening programs such as the Provincial Nominee Program, and improving foreign credential recognition. We remain committed to working closely with our federal partners and provincial and territorial colleagues to ensure newcomers are supported and that Canada’s immigration system continues to deliver meaningful outcomes across the country.”

– The Honourable Lin Paddock, Minister of Immigration for Newfoundland and Labrador and Provincial-Territorial FMRI Co-Chair

Contacts

Contacts for media only

Taous Ait Senior Communications Advisor and Press Secretary Office of the Minister Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Taous.Ait@cic.gc.ca

Media Relations People and Communications Sector Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada media@cic.gc.ca

Brodie Thomas Energy and Mines Government of Newfoundland and Labrador 709-729-5777, 709-725-3759 brodiethomas@gov.nl.ca

items.bodyZh:
加拿大移民、难民和公民部发布

新闻稿

移民部长论坛(FMRI: Forum of Ministers Responsible for Immigration)今日召开会议，推进2027-2029年移民配额计划(Immigration Levels Plan)的合作，讨论联邦-省-地区(FPT: Federal–Provincial–Territorial)优先事项。与会部长讨论了在考虑区域、农村和北方背景下将移民恢复到可持续水平的问题，并探讨了如何解决劳动力短缺、强化关键经济部门以及支持全国各社区的方式。部长们还讨论了省提名项目(PNPs: Provincial Nominee Programs)在推进这些举措中的作用，以及对稳定和充足的PNP配额的需求。

2026年6月23日——渥太华——移民部长论坛(FMRI)今日召开会议，推进2027-2029年移silon配额计划的合作，讨论联邦-省-地区(FPT)优先事项。与会部长讨论了在考虑区域、农村和北方背景下将移民恢复到可持续水平的问题，并探讨了如何解决劳动力短缺、强化关键经济部门以及支持全国各社区的方式。部长们还讨论了省提名项目(PNPs)在推进这些举措中的作用，以及对稳定和充足的PNP配额的需求。

部长们在进行前瞻性讨论的同时，还审查了最近改革的实施情况，包括支持可持续移民和加强项目完整性的措施。部长们同意，有意义的协商、谨慎的规划以及在永久和临时移民途径上进行的有效管理，对于恢复加拿大移民制度的平衡并与经济目标保持一致至关重要。部长们讨论了联邦承诺在2027年后将永久居民入境人数稳定在总人口的1%以下，以及在2027年底前将临时人口减少到总人口的5%以下，并同意需要继续取得进展以将移民恢复到可持续水平。

部长们强调，移民是一个共同管辖权事项，各省和地区需要作为实质性伙伴参与移民配额和项目的制定，以提高对加拿大各地不同劳动力市场和人口需求的响应能力。部长们同意了联邦-省-地区伙伴之间以及与战略利益相关者进行强有力合作的重要性，同时确保新移民在国家和区域层面关键劳动力部门的成功融合。他们强调，吸引和留住世界上具有促进国家和区域经济增长所需技能和经验的最优秀人才，是实现加拿大经济目标的关键。省地区部长要求获得更多关于设定PNP目标和配额流程的信息，以便进行更具战略性的政策和项目规划。

部长们讨论了改进经济移民项目的问题，包括现代化流程、精简工作流程和减少候选人选择的重复。他们讨论了改进快速通道系统(Express Entry system)以更好地满足区域劳动力市场需求和新移民结果的方式。省地区部长强调，PNP和其他区域经济项目(如大西洋移民项目(AIP: Atlantic Immigration Program))是应对关键部门地区劳动力短缺的最有效工具，使省和地区能够直接选择符合其经济优先事项的技术工人。省地区部长强调了增加PNP和AIP配额的重要性，以确保省和地区对经济移民有更大的控制权。

部长们强调了需要在移民项目和举措之间加强互补性，以便促进已在加拿大的工人和国际学生从临时居留向永久居留的过渡，同时确保新入境者的可持续水平。他们重申，这些协调方式需要通过协调一致的定居服务和项目以整体方式支持移民融合。部长们强调了省和地区在新移民融合中的作用。省地区部长敦促联邦政府恢复在针对性语言培训等领域的充分和相应资金水平，以支持新移民的持续定居需求，帮助他们取得成功。

部长们讨论了吸引和招聘国际学生以支持加拿大经济的重要性。省地区部长同意进一步合作以塑造毕业后工作签证项目(Post-Graduation Work Permit Program)，使其更好地与区域劳动力市场需求保持一致。

部长们肯定了在减少外国学历认可(FCR: foreign credential recognition)障碍方面的先前努力和取得的进展，包括特定职业改革(特别是在医疗保健方面)、FCR系统改革，以及在大多数司法管辖区引入公平注册惯例立法。他们指示官员通过新建立的劳动力市场部长论坛-移民部长论坛联合工作小组(Forum of Labour Market Ministers - FMRI Joint Task Force)迅速协力工作，以采取协调一致的方式解决整个移民过程(从抵达前到就业)中的挑战和障碍。

部长们表示支持法语移民作为优先事项，认可它在加强魁北克以外的法语少数民族社区中所能发挥的作用，包括支持他们融入我们的社区。他们肯定了为帮助实现加拿大政府到2029年将法语永久居民入境人数达到12%的目标而正在开展的这项工作。他们讨论了制定经更新的联邦/省/地区行动计划的进展情况，以增加魁北克以外地区的法语移民，并欢迎与加拿大法语社区部长理事会(Ministers' Council on the Canadian Francophonie)的合作和互补，以推进这项工作。

部长们共同承诺在所有移民政策和项目上进行密切、有意义和及时的合作，以确保为加拿大和加拿大人获得最佳可能的成果。

注：根据魁北克政府的要求，负责移民的魁北克部长在联邦和省-地区部长会议上享有观察员身份。在《加拿大-魁北克协议》(Canada-Québec Accord)下，魁北克对确定移民水平以及移民的选择、法语化和融合承担全面责任。在其权责范围内，魁北克制定其政策和项目，并进行立法、监管和制定其自己的标准。魁北克不是讨论的一方，也不受关于与外国学历认可相关的泛加拿大举措的决定的约束。

关于FMRI：移民部长论坛是一个联邦-省-地区决策机构，目标是支持加拿大建立灵活、及时和有效的移民制度。该论坛的使命是推进共同的移民优先事项，增强加拿大的移民政策和项目。

语录

"加拿大人期望他们的政府负责任地管理移民，在可持续性和制度完整性与努力吸引世界最优秀人才之间取得平衡。与我们的伙伴一起，我们现在正在开展这项工作。通过恢复对我们制度的信心和控制，我们将最大化新移民对加拿大各地社区能够带来的社会和经济效益。我期待在未来的几个月和几年里在我们的共同优先事项上进一步合作。"

——尊敬的莱娜·梅特雷奇·迪亚布(Lena Metlege Diab)部长，加拿大移民、难民和公民部

"省和地区在塑造反映区域劳动力市场需求、支持经济增长和帮助各种规模社区繁荣发展的移民途径方面发挥着至关重要的作用。我对我们在许多问题上取得的进展感到鼓舞，包括推进移民配额合作、加强省提名项目等项目，以及改进外国学历认可。我们仍然致力于与联邦伙伴以及省和地区同事密切合作，以确保新移民得到支持，加拿大的移民制度继续在全国范围内提供有意义的成果。"

——尊敬的林·帕多克(Lin Paddock)部长，纽芬兰和拉布拉多移民部长及省-地区移民部长论坛联席主席

联系方式

仅供媒体联系

塔乌斯·艾特(Taous Ait) 高级传讯顾问兼新闻秘书 加拿大移民、难民和公民部部长办公室 Taous.Ait@cic.gc.ca

媒体关系 加拿大移民、难民和公民部人员和通讯部门 media@cic.gc.ca

布罗迪·托马斯(Brodie Thomas) 能源和矿产 纽芬兰和拉布拉多政府 709-729-5777, 709-725-3759 brodiethomas@gov.nl.ca

页面详情
items.summaryZh: 加拿大联邦、省和地区移民部长召开会议，讨论可持续移民政策。会议重点涉及2027-2029年移民配额计划、将永久居民比例控制在总人口1%以下、临时人口比例降至5%以下，以及省提名项目(PNP)和大西洋移民项目(AIP)的配额分配等问题。这些决定将直接影响未来三年希望移居加拿大的申请人及各省的移民选择权。

items.title: 110 Saskatchewan Youth Apprenticeship Industry Scholarships Awarded to High School Students For 2025-26
items.bodyEn:
Released on June 22, 2026

The Saskatchewan Apprenticeship and Trade Certification Commission (SATCC) has awarded Saskatchewan Youth Apprenticeship (SYA) Industry Scholarships to 110 high school students from 68 communities across the province.

Each scholarship is worth $1,000 and can be redeemed once students meet specific requirements. To redeem the scholarship, students must, within two years of completing SYA, either register as apprentices in Saskatchewan and complete Level 1 technical training, or complete a recognized pre-apprenticeship program in a designated trade in Saskatchewan.

"Congratulations to the deserving recipients of this year's SYA Industry Scholarships," Immigration and Career Training Minister Eric Schmalz said. "Skilled tradespeople are in high demand in Saskatchewan and essential to the growth of our province. Our government is committed to ensuring more young people pursue rewarding careers in the skilled trades."

Every year, the SATCC asks SYA Champions, the educators who oversee the SYA program in their schools, to nominate outstanding students participating in SYA for the scholarship. SYA Champions are encouraged to nominate students who have taken steps toward building a skilled trades career and plan to actively pursue a skilled trades career following graduation.

One of this year's recipients is Emersyn Graham. She is graduating from Ernie Studer School in Loon Lake and is planning to pursue a career as a Powerline Technician.

"I am so grateful to have been chosen out of the many qualified candidates for the Saskatchewan Youth Apprenticeship Industry Scholarship," Graham said. "Winning this makes me feel closer to achieving my goals in the trades field and sets me up for the next stages of my career to become an apprentice Powerline Technician. The efforts of SYA help people to understand that the success of the past, present, and future lies in the trades. I feel everyone should be a part of that, which is why I am so thankful to be starting my life with something that can positively impact everyone's future."

"The SYA Industry Scholarship is a great bonus for youth entering the skilled trades in Saskatchewan," SATCC CEO Jeff Ritter said. "New apprenticeship registrations are increasing, and this is a positive indicator for high school graduates looking to start their careers in the skilled trades. Congratulations to this year's scholarship winners."

Since the scholarship's launch in 2009, more than 1,500 scholarships have been issued. Sixty industry sponsors, including employers, industry associations and unions; along with the Government of Saskatchewan, have invested a total of $1.2 million into the SYA Industry Scholarship fund.

There are currently more than 2,500 students registered in SYA. Nearly 40 per cent of SYA graduates register as apprentices in Saskatchewan within five years of completing the program.

For more information on the SYA program, please visit: saskapprenticeship.ca/sask-youth-apprenticeship .

items.bodyZh:
标题：萨省为2025-26学年110名高中学生颁发青年学徒产业奖学金

发布日期：2026年6月22日

萨省学徒和贸易认证委员会（Saskatchewan Apprenticeship and Trade Certification Commission, SATCC）已向来自全省68个社区的110名高中学生颁发了萨省青年学徒（Saskatchewan Youth Apprenticeship, SYA）产业奖学金。

每份奖学金价值1000加元，学生在满足特定要求后可兑现。为了兑现奖学金，学生必须在完成SYA后的两年内，在萨省注册为学徒并完成一级技能培训，或在萨省完成指定行业的认可预学徒项目。

"祝贺今年SYA产业奖学金的应得获奖者，"移民和职业培训部长埃里克·施马尔茨（Eric Schmalz）表示。"技工在萨省需求旺盛，对我省的增长至关重要。我们政府致力于确保更多年轻人从事技工行业的有意义职业。"

每年，SATCC会要求SYA项目倡导者（在各校负责监督SYA项目的教育工作者）提名参与SYA的优秀学生竞争该奖学金。SYA项目倡导者被鼓励提名那些已为技工职业生涯做出努力并计划毕业后积极追求技工职业的学生。

今年的获奖者之一是埃默瑟恩·格雷厄姆（Emersyn Graham）。她即将从卢恩湖的欧尼·斯图德学校（Ernie Studer School）毕业，计划从事电力线技术员职业。

"我很感谢在众多合格候选人中被选中获得萨省青年学徒产业奖学金，"格雷厄姆说。"赢得这个奖学金让我感到更接近实现我在技工领域的目标，为我成为电力线学徒的下一阶段职业生涯做好了准备。SYA的努力帮助人们理解，过去、现在和未来的成功在于技工行业。我认为每个人都应该参与其中，这就是为什么我很感谢能够以对每个人的未来都能产生积极影响的事业开启我的人生。"

"SYA产业奖学金对进入萨省技工行业的青年来说是一个很好的奖励，"SATCC首席执行官杰夫·里特（Jeff Ritter）表示。"新的学徒注册在增加，这对希望在技工行业开启职业生涯的高中毕业生来说是一个积极信号。祝贺今年的奖学金获得者。"

自2009年奖学金启动以来，已颁发了1500多份奖学金。包括雇主、行业协会和工会在内的60个产业赞助商，以及萨省政府，已向SYA产业奖学金基金投入总计120万加元。

目前有超过2500名学生注册参加SYA项目。约40%的SYA项目毕业生在完成该项目后的五年内在萨省注册为学徒。

如需了解更多关于SYA项目的信息，请访问：saskapprenticeship.ca/sask-youth-apprenticeship。
items.summaryZh: 萨省为110名高中生颁发青年学徒产业奖学金，每份1000加元，学生需在完成项目后两年内注册为学徒并完成一级技能培训，或完成指定行业的预学徒项目才能兑现。此举旨在吸引更多年轻人进入萨省紧缺的技工行业。

Minister Diab will be joined by the Honourable Lin Paddock, Minister of Immigration for Newfoundland and Labrador and Provincial-Territorial FMRI Co-Chair.

items.title: Canada marks World Refugee Day and reflects on 75 years of refugee protection
items.bodyEn:
From: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

Statement

On World Refugee Day, Canada recognizes the 75th anniversary of the 1951 Refugee Convention and the international refugee protection system it created, including the principle that people have the right to seek safety from persecution.

Ottawa, June 20, 2026 —The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship, issued the following statement today:

“On World Refugee Day , Canada recognizes the 75th anniversary of the 1951 Refugee Convention and the international refugee protection system it created, including the principle that people have the right to seek safety from persecution.

For 75 years, this framework has helped shape how countries support people forced to flee conflict and crisis. Canadians have a long-standing and proud humanitarian tradition. It is part of who we are. It reflects our values and our international commitments. Today, the world is facing a shared challenge: how to protect the rights and well-being of increasing numbers of people, so that they can live and work freely.

That is why Canada’s humanitarian commitment matters. When people are offered safety, stability and the opportunity to rebuild their lives, they become neighbours, workers, students, parents, volunteers and community leaders. They make valuable social, cultural and economic contributions, helping to build our communities and shape the country we share.

Canada will continue to support people in need while strengthening the integrity of our protection programs. We are doing this by taking a responsible path that upholds our humanitarian tradition and allows us to work with partners to strengthen refugee protection, while keeping the system fair, well managed and sustainable.

People have always moved to find safety, opportunity, family and a future. Today, and every day, we reaffirm our commitment to those seeking safety, and to the partnerships needed to protect people, strengthen communities and help build lasting solutions.”

For further information (media only), please contact:

Taous Ait Senior Communications Advisor and Press Secretary Office of the Minister Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Taous.Ait@cic.gc.ca

Page details
items.bodyZh:
来源：加拿大移民、难民和公民身份部

声明

在世界难民日，加拿大纪念《1951年难民公约》（1951 Refugee Convention）成立75周年及其创建的国际难民保护制度，该制度确立了人民有权寻求安全庇护以逃离迫害的原则。

2026年6月20日，渥太华——加拿大移民、难民和公民身份部部长尊敬的莱娜·梅特莱格·迪亚卜（The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab）今日发布以下声明：

"在世界难民日，加拿大纪念《1951年难民公约》成立75周年及其创建的国际难民保护制度，该制度确立了人民有权寻求安全庇护以逃离迫害的原则。

75年来，这一框架帮助塑造了各国如何支持被迫逃离冲突和危机的人民的方式。加拿大拥有悠久而光荣的人道主义传统。这是我们身份的组成部分。它反映了我们的价值观和国际承诺。今天，世界面临共同挑战：如何保护越来越多人的权利和福祉，使他们能够自由地生活和工作。

正因如此，加拿大的人道主义承诺至关重要。当人民获得安全、稳定和重建生活的机会时，他们会成为邻居、劳动者、学生、父母、志愿者和社区领袖。他们做出宝贵的社会、文化和经济贡献，帮助建设我们的社区和塑造我们共同的国家。

加拿大将继续支持需要帮助的人，同时加强我们保护项目的完整性。我们通过采取负责任的路径来做到这一点，该路径既维护我们的人道主义传统，又使我们能够与伙伴合作加强难民保护，同时保持制度的公平、高效和可持续。

人类历来为寻求安全、机遇、家庭和未来而迁移。今天及每一天，我们重申对寻求安全者的承诺，以及对保护人民、加强社区和帮助建立持久解决方案所需伙伴关系的承诺。"

如需进一步信息（仅限媒体），请联系：

陶乌斯·艾特（Taous Ait）资深通讯顾问兼新闻发言人
加拿大移民、难民和公民身份部部长办公室
Taous.Ait@cic.gc.ca

页面详情

items.title: Canada proposes new regulations to modernize the asylum process and support timely decisions
items.bodyEn:
From: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

News release

Building on recent measures to strengthen Canada’s immigration and asylum systems, and recent decreases in asylum claim volumes, the Government of Canada is taking further action to maintain a fair, orderly and well-managed asylum system.

June 19, 2026—Ottawa— Building on recent measures to strengthen Canada’s immigration and asylum systems, and recent decreases in asylum claim volumes, the Government of Canada is taking further action to maintain a fair, orderly and well-managed asylum system.

Today, the Government of Canada published proposed regulations to implement recent asylum reforms introduced through the Strengthening Canada’s Immigration System and Borders Act . Canadians, stakeholders and interested organizations are invited to review the proposed regulations and provide feedback during a 30-day consultation period, with implementation anticipated later in 2026.

The changes would establish clear requirements and timelines to improve how asylum claims are received, processed and decided. The measures are also designed to make the asylum process faster so that claims are processed more effectively while Canada’s commitment to protecting those in need continues to be upheld.

The proposed regulations would simplify and streamline the claim process and

• clarify the asylum application process

• establish timelines for key government review

• specify rules for reinstatement of withdrawn claims and claims that are not abandoned

• strengthen support for vulnerable claimants

• help eligible claimants get access to work permits sooner

• create exceptions to new ineligibility rules

These changes build on recent measures to reduce pressures on the asylum system, improve efficiency and strengthen system integrity, while continuing to provide timely protection to people who need it most.

Quotes

“A well-managed asylum system benefits everyone. These reforms help people who need protection receive it sooner, provide greater certainty, and give them the opportunity to build their lives and contribute to communities across Canada. They also support a fair, efficient and sustainable asylum system by facilitating faster decisions for people who don’t qualify for protection.”

– The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship

Quick facts

• On March 26, 2026, Bill C-12 received royal assent and became law, strengthening Canada’s immigration and asylum systems in 4 key areas: new eligibility requirements for asylum claims a modernized asylum process improved domestic information sharing expanded immigration document and application authorities As part of the IRCC Red Tape Review , IRCC identified opportunities to simplify asylum processes and improve the efficiency and integrity of the overall system. From January to April 2026, 42% fewer people submitted an asylum claim in Canada compared to the same period in 2025. That’s 63% fewer claimants compared to the same period in 2024.

Related products

• Canada proposes new regulations to modernize the asylum process and support timely decisions - Canada.ca

Associated links

• Canada Gazette, Part 1

• New immigration and asylum measures from Bill C-12 (the Strengthening Canada’s Immigration System and Borders Act) have become law – Canada.ca

Contacts

Contacts for media only

Media Relations People and Communications Sector Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada media@cic.gc.ca
