items.title: Saskatchewan Invests $3.5 Million in Regina and Saskatoon Trades and Skills Centres
items.bodyEn:
Released on May 5, 2026

The Government of Saskatchewan has increased funding by $534,000 to the Trades and Skills Centres in Saskatoon and Regina, bringing the total investment to $3.5 million. This increase will fund three additional programs at both Trades and Skills Centres.

"Our government is making targeted investments to protect our workforce in the province," Immigration and Career Training Minister Eric Schmalz said. "Investments into the Regina and Saskatoon Trades and Skills Center help Saskatchewan employers fill the skilled jobs being created by our strong and growing economy."

The Regina and Saskatoon Trades and Skills Centres deliver short-term demand driven trades and skills programs that lead to entry-level jobs. These training centres work with employers and industry to provide hands-on training experiences that meet the needs of learners, employers and the labour market.

The Regina Trades and Skills Centre enrolled 174 students in 2024-25.

"We are incredibly grateful for the Government of Saskatchewan's continued investment in Regina Trades and Skills Centre" RTSC Executive Director Brian Shankowsky said. "This funding allows us to expand our programming and respond directly to the needs of local employers, while giving more individuals the opportunity to gain meaningful, hands-on training. At RTSC, our focus is on helping people build confidence, develop skills and secure employment - and this support plays a key role in making that happen. This funding not only strengthens our capacity to move forward with confidence but also reinforces the importance of collaboration in building a stronger and more connected community. We are proud to be part of strengthening Saskatchewan's workforce and economy."

The Saskatoon Trades and Skills Centre enrolled 138 students in 2024-25.

"Entry level employees are in demand in industries across the province; this additional funding from the Government of Saskatchewan will allow us to offer an additional three demand led industry training programs a year," Saskatoon Trades and Skills Centre Executive Director Karen Cederwall said. "This funding allows us to help Saskatchewan residents access training that meets their needs and the needs of employers. We would like to thank the Government of Saskatchewan for recognizing the important work we do which contributes to the growth and sustainability of our province.

The Government of Saskatchewan works with training providers like the Regina and Saskatoon Trade and Skills Centres to help prepare Saskatchewan people for jobs. These targeted investments into training programs support Building the Workforce for a Growing Economy: The Saskatchewan Labour Market Strategy , which outlines how the province is ensuring Saskatchewan has the labour force it needs to support employer demand and that Saskatchewan people benefit from the province's growing economy.

-30-
items.bodyZh:
萨省投资350万加元支持里贾纳和萨斯卡通技工与技能中心

发布于2026年5月5日

萨省政府向萨斯卡通和里贾纳的技工与技能中心（Trades and Skills Centres）增加了534,000加元的资金，使总投资达到350万加元。这笔增资将为两个技工与技能中心各资助三个额外项目。

"我们政府正在做出有针对性的投资，以保护本省的劳动力，"移民和职业培训部长埃里克·施马尔茨（Eric Schmalz）表示。"对里贾纳和萨斯卡通技工与技能中心的投资有助于萨省雇主填补我们强劲增长的经济所创造的技能工作岗位。"

里贾纳和萨斯卡通技工与技能中心提供短期、需求驱动的技工和技能培训项目，这些项目可帮助学员获得入门级工作。这些培训中心与雇主和行业合作，提供实践培训体验，以满足学员、雇主和劳动力市场的需求。

里贾纳技工与技能中心在2024-25年度招收了174名学生。

"我们对萨省政府对里贾纳技工与技能中心的持续投资感到非常感谢，"里贾纳技工与技能中心执行主任布赖恩·尚科夫斯基（Brian Shankowsky）表示。"这笔资金使我们能够扩展课程项目，直接应对本地雇主的需求，同时为更多个人提供获得有意义的实践培训的机会。在RTSC，我们的重点是帮助人们建立信心、发展技能并获得就业——这种支持在实现这一目标方面发挥了关键作用。这笔资金不仅加强了我们自信前行的能力，而且强调了协作在建设更强大、更紧密相连的社区中的重要性。我们荣幸能够成为加强萨省劳动力和经济的一部分。"

萨斯卡通技工与技能中心在2024-25年度招收了138名学生。

"入门级员工在全省各行业都有需求；萨省政府提供的这笔额外资金将使我们每年能够提供三个额外的需求驱动型行业培训项目，"萨斯卡通技工与技能中心执行主任卡伦·塞德沃尔（Karen Cederwall）表示。"这笔资金使我们能够帮助萨省居民获得满足其需求和雇主需求的培训。我们要感谢萨省政府认可我们所从事的重要工作，该工作对本省的增长和可持续性做出了贡献。"

萨省政府与里贾纳和萨斯卡通技工与技能中心等培训提供商合作，帮助为萨省人民准备工作机会。这些对培训项目的有针对性投资支持《为增长型经济培养劳动力：萨省劳动力市场战略》（Building the Workforce for a Growing Economy: The Saskatchewan Labour Market Strategy），该战略阐述了萨省如何确保拥有满足雇主需求的劳动力，以及萨省人民从本省增长的经济中受益。

-30-

items.title: Understanding the one-time In-Canada Workers Initiative
items.bodyEn:
From: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

Backgrounder

The In-Canada Workers Initiative will accelerate permanent residence applications in our inventories for individuals who are already contributing to Canada’s economy across a wide range of in-demand sectors, including in rural areas and communities with labour gaps. Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) will continue to process applications within existing levels space for workers who have applied for permanent residence but are not eligible under this initiative.

The In-Canada Workers Initiative will accelerate permanent residence applications in our inventories for individuals who are already contributing to Canada’s economy across a wide range of in-demand sectors, including in small and rural areas and communities with labour gaps. Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) will continue to process applications within existing levels space for workers who have applied for permanent residence but are not eligible under this initiative.

Accelerating applications in existing inventories

IRCC is initially accelerating applications that are already selected through well‑established regional immigration programs led by provinces, territories and community partners, as well as occupation-driven pilots.

The Provincial Nominee Program lets provinces and territories nominate people who have the skills, education and work experience to help their economy and who want to live in that province or territory. Each province and territory has its own requirements.

The Atlantic Immigration Program is a pathway to permanent residence for skilled foreign workers and international graduates from a Canadian institution who want to work and live in one of Canada’s 4 Atlantic provinces—New Brunswick, Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island or Newfoundland and Labrador.

The community immigration pilots offer permanent residence to skilled candidates who want to work and live in one of 18 selected Canadian communities. Employers can contact their community to get designated and hire in priority sectors.

The caregiver pilots provide pathways to permanent residence for qualified home child care workers and home support workers. The Department continues to process applications received through to a final decision.

The Agri-Food Pilot helps address the labour needs of the Canadian agriculture and agri­food sector. Although this pilot has concluded, IRCC continues to process applications accepted before May 14, 2025.

Focusing on areas essential to local economies and smaller community sustainability

This initiative is designed to support Canada’s economic growth by processing applications from individuals who are already contributing in a wide range of critical sectors facing ongoing labour shortages, particularly in smaller communities.

We are focusing immigration where it delivers the greatest impact—filling labour gaps, strengthening key sectors of the economy and supporting the economic vitality of Canada’s smaller communities across the country.

Page details
items.bodyZh:
来源:加拿大移民、难民和公民部

背景说明

在加工作者倡议(In-Canada Workers Initiative)将加快我们库存中的永久居民申请处理,这些申请来自已在加拿大经济中做出贡献的个人,他们分布在各种紧缺行业,包括农村地区和存在劳动力缺口的社区。加拿大移民、难民和公民部(IRCC)将继续在现有名额空间内处理已申请永久居民但不符合该倡议资格的工作者的申请。

在加工作者倡议(In-Canada Workers Initiative)将加快我们库存中的永久居民申请处理,这些申请来自已在加拿大经济中做出贡献的个人,他们分布在各种紧缺行业,包括小型和农村地区以及存在劳动力缺口的社区。加拿大移民、难民和公民部(IRCC)将继续在现有名额空间内处理已申请永久居民但不符合该倡议资格的工作者的申请。

加快库存中申请的处理

IRCC初步加快处理通过成熟区域移民项目已获提名的申请,这些项目由各省、各地区和社区伙伴主导,以及由职业驱动的试点项目。

省提名计划(Provincial Nominee Program)允许各省和地区提名具有有助于当地经济发展的技能、教育和工作经验、并希望在该省或地区生活的人士。各省和地区均有各自的要求。

大西洋移民计划(Atlantic Immigration Program)是一条面向希望在加拿大4个大西洋省份——新不伦瑞克省、新斯科舍省、爱德华王子岛或纽芬兰与拉布拉多省工作和生活的技术移民和加拿大机构国际毕业生的永久居民途径。

社区移民试点(community immigration pilots)为希望在18个指定加拿大社区之一工作和生活的技术人才提供永久居民途径。雇主可以联系其所在社区以获得指定资格并在优先行业招聘。

护理工作试点(caregiver pilots)为符合条件的家庭托儿工作者和居家支持工作者提供永久居民途径。该部门继续处理通过该项目提交的申请直至做出最终决定。

农业食品试点(Agri-Food Pilot)有助于应对加拿大农业和农食部门的劳动力需求。尽管该试点已结束,IRCC继续处理2025年5月14日前获批的申请。

聚焦对当地经济和小型社区可持续发展至关重要的领域

该倡议旨在通过处理已在广泛的关键部门(面临持续劳动力短缺,特别是在小型社区)做出贡献的个人的申请,支持加拿大的经济增长。

我们将移民政策的重点放在能产生最大影响之处——填补劳动力缺口、加强经济关键部门、支持加拿大全国小型社区的经济活力。

页面详情
items.summaryZh: 加拿大推出"一次性在加工作者倡议"(In-Canada Workers Initiative),将加快已在该国经济中做出贡献的在职工人的永久居民申请处理,覆盖多个紧缺行业及农村和劳动力短缺地区。这一举措旨在通过省提名项目、大西洋移民计划、社区移民试点和护理工作试点等既有渠道,优先处理库存中的申请,以支持加拿大经济增长和小型社区的可持续发展。

items.title: Filling labour gaps in smaller communities by accelerating permanent residence for 33,000 workers
items.bodyEn:
From: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

News release

Canada continues to bring our immigration system to sustainable levels and focus immigration on the areas where it has the greatest impact. This means supporting rural and remote communities that are experiencing labour shortages in key economic sectors.

May 4, 2026 — Ottawa, Ontario — Canada continues to bring its immigration system to sustainable levels while focussing immigration on the areas where it has the greatest impact. This includes supporting rural and remote communities that are experiencing labour shortages in key economic sectors.

Today, the Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship, announced progress on the department’s one-time In-Canada Workers Initiative, as announced in Budget 2025, which accelerates the transition of up to 33,000 workers in Canada to permanent residence in 2026 and 2027. These individuals have already established strong roots in their communities and are contributing to a stronger Canadian economy.

As part of this initiative, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) is initially accelerating eligible applications from existing inventories of work permit holders who have applied for permanent residence. These eligible workers are already supporting their smaller and rural communities’ labour and economic needs, and they

• have applied through the Provincial Nominee Program, the Atlantic Immigration Program, the community immigration pilots, the caregiver pilots or the Agri­Food Pilot; and

• have been living in smaller communities in Canada for 2 years or more

Through these criteria, IRCC will grant permanent residence to applicants across a range of in-demand sectors in rural areas and communities with labour gaps.

Progress on the In-Canada Workers Initiative is tracked on our website and will be updated monthly.

Quotes

“This initiative is designed to promote economic growth and address labour shortages in key sectors where they are most needed—in smaller Canadian communities. By transitioning temporary residents who are already living and contributing to their communities to permanent residence, we’re providing the certainty and the stability needed to maintain and grow vibrant local economies.”

– The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship

“Rural and remote communities are at the heart of our plan to build One Canadian Economy, , and our new government is listening to their distinct needs and realities. By allowing members of these communities to transition to permanent residence, we are supporting the growth and vitality of our rural and remote communities while also helping them address labour shortages in key sectors over the long term.”

– The Honourable Buckley Bélanger, Secretary of State (Rural Development)

Quick facts

• IRCC is aiming to transition at least 20,000 workers to permanent residence in 2026 and the remainder in 2027. We are making progress and are on track to meet these goals. Between January 1st and February 28, 2026, 3,600 workers were granted permanent residence under this initiative. Eligible applicants being granted permanent residence were already selected through occupation driven pilots and well established regional immigration programs led by provinces, territories and community partners, as well as occupation driven pilots. Applicants do not need to take any action. The one-time In-Canada Workers Initiative was announced in Budget 2025 and will help reduce existing permanent residents inventories to help ensure that workers with ties to Canada are able to continue contributing in the long term. This initiative supports the government’s ongoing efforts to reduce the share of temporary residents to less than 5% of the population by the end of 2027.

• New immigration measure to support Quebec workers and employers

• News release – Canada prioritizes top talent in 2026 immigration Express Entry categories

• News release – Canada exceeded its Francophone immigration target for 2025

• News release – Canada invests in Francophone communities and helps build their future by attracting top global talent

Page details
items.bodyZh:
来源：加拿大移民、难民和公民部

新闻稿

加拿大继续推动移民制度走向可持续水平，并将移民重点聚焦在影响最大的地区。这意味着支持在关键经济部门经历劳动力短缺的农村和偏远社区。

2026年5月4日——加拿大安大略省渥太华——加拿大继续推动其移民制度走向可持续水平，同时将移民重点聚焦在影响最大的地区。这包括支持在关键经济部门经历劳动力短缺的农村和偏远社区。

今日，移民、难民和公民部长莱纳·梅特莱奇·迪亚布（Lena Metlege Diab）宣布了该部门的一次性《加拿大在境内工作者倡议》(In-Canada Workers Initiative)取得进展，该倡议在2025年预算案中公布，将在2026年和2027年加速多达33,000名工作者向永久居民身份的过渡。这些人员已在各自的社区中建立了深厚的根基，并为加拿大经济增长做出贡献。

作为该倡议的一部分，加拿大移民、难民和公民部(IRCC)最初从已申请永久居民身份的工作许可证持有人现有存档中加速符合条件的申请。这些符合条件的工作者已在支持其所在小型和农村社区的劳动力及经济需求，并且

• 已通过省提名项目(Provincial Nominee Program)、大西洋移民项目(Atlantic Immigration Program)、社区移民试点项目、护理人员试点项目或农业食品试点项目(Agri-Food Pilot)提出申请；以及

• 已在加拿大小型社区居住2年或以上

通过这些标准，IRCC将向农村地区和存在劳动力缺口社区的多个高需求部门的申请人授予永久居民身份。

《加拿大在境内工作者倡议》的进展将在我们的网站上追踪，并每月更新。

引用评论

"该倡议旨在促进经济增长并解决关键部门的劳动力短缺问题——这些短缺最为迫切的是在加拿大较小的社区。通过将已在其社区生活和贡献力量的临时居民过渡到永久居民身份，我们为维持和发展充满活力的地方经济所需的确定性和稳定性提供了保障。"

——移民、难民和公民部长莱纳·梅特莱奇·迪亚布

"农村和偏远社区是我们建设《一个加拿大经济》计划的核心，我们的新政府正在倾听这些社区的独特需求和现实。通过允许这些社区的成员过渡到永久居民身份，我们正在支持农村和偏远社区的增长和活力，同时也在帮助他们长期解决关键部门的劳动力短缺问题。"

——农村发展国务秘书巴克利·贝朗格(Buckley Bélanger)

快速事实

• IRCC的目标是在2026年至少将20,000名工作者过渡为永久居民，其余的在2027年完成。我们正在取得进展，并有望实现这些目标。在2026年1月1日至2月28日期间，3,600名工作者已根据该倡议获得永久居民身份。获得永久居民身份的符合条件的申请人已通过职业驱动型试点项目和由各省、领地和社区合作伙伴主导的建立完善的区域移民项目以及职业驱动型试点项目遴选。申请人无需采取任何行动。一次性《加拿大在境内工作者倡议》在2025年预算案中宣布，将有助于减少现有永久居民申请积压，确保与加拿大有联系的工作者能够继续长期贡献。该倡议支持政府到2027年底前将临时居民比例降至人口总数5%以下的持续努力。

items.title: Minister Diab highlights sport investments from Spring Economic Update 2026: Canada Strong for All to build stronger and safer communities
items.bodyEn:
From: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

News release

In Budget 2025, the Government of Canada outlined its plan to build Canada strong. Since then, we have moved fast to build the major infrastructure, homes and industries that grow Canada’s economy and create lasting prosperity; empower Canadians with better careers and a more affordable life; and protect our communities, our borders and our way of life.

May 2, 2026—Halifax – In Budget 2025, the Government of Canada outlined its plan to build Canada strong. Since then, we have moved fast to build the major infrastructure, homes and industries that grow Canada’s economy and create lasting prosperity; empower Canadians with better careers and a more affordable life; and protect our communities, our borders and our way of life.

We delivered concrete savings for Canadians while supporting key national priorities and keeping investments focused on results. We are maintaining a strong fiscal position, with the Spring Economic Update 2026 showing that projected deficits are lower over the fiscal horizon and that we are on track to meet our fiscal anchors

The Spring Economic Update 2026 is the next step in our plan to build Canada Strong for All. It provides a clear update on the strength of Canada’s economy, giving Canadians confidence in our plan. It delivers targeted relief to make life more affordable, support workers, and accelerate the construction of homes and major infrastructure. It also strengthens Canada’s competitiveness and economic growth while investing in strong, safe communities across the country.

Today, the Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship, highlighted key sport investments from the Spring Economic Update to build stronger and safer communities.

The Government of Canada is investing $755 million to expand access to sport, creating opportunities for Canadians to build meaningful relationships in their communities, making full use of existing and new infrastructure, and supporting Canada's world class athletes who inspire pride and unity, as we celebrate their accomplishments as a country.

As part of this investment, the Mayflower Curling Club will receive $4 million to support the construction of a new, modern, accessible facility. This project will help grow the sport, remove barriers to participation and create an inclusive space where Canadians of all backgrounds and abilities can get involved and stay active.

Canada’s new government is transforming our economy from reliance to resilience. The Spring Economic Update 2026 ensures all Canadians can participate in building Canada strong and share in its success. Other key measures include:

• The Canada Strong Fund— Canada’s first national sovereign wealth fund. This will invest in key, strategic Canadian projects and companies. While Canadians will benefit from these nation-building projects through jobs, economic growth and greater security, the government is determined to ensure that Canadians also have a stake in the projects themselves. That’s why a unique and important feature of the Canada Strong Fund will be its new retail investment product. This allows Canadians to receive financial returns as we build Canada strong together.

• Team Canada Strong— a new nationwide effort to recruit, train and hire 80,000 to 100,000 new skilled trade workers by 2030–31. This initiative creates new opportunities for Canadians and attracts the workers needed to build more homes and major projects at speed and at scale.

• Building Stronger Communities— by making communities safer, more connected and more resilient. We are building more homes, getting tougher on crime and fraud, and funding essential infrastructure, including small-craft harbours that sustain coastal communities and local jobs. We are also investing to build healthier, safer and stronger Indigenous communities.

Canada’s new government is building a Canada that is not just strong, but good; not just prosperous, but fair; a Canada that is not just for some, most of the time, but for all, at all times. We’re building Canada strong for all.

Quotes

“The Spring Economic Update 2026 builds on the momentum of our budget, combining strategic investments with sustained fiscal discipline to keep building Canada Strong for All – delivering prosperity today and strengthening our economy for tomorrow. At this pivotal moment in Canada’s history, we’re charting a course through the fog of uncertainty and global headwinds with strength, determination and ambition—and building one strong Canadian economy, by Canadians, for Canadians.”

—The Honourable François-Philippe Champagne, Minister of Finance and National Revenue

“This is a generational investment in sport because sport is about more than competition. It is about belonging, physical activity, confidence and opportunity. From the playground to the podium, we are expanding access to sport so more Canadians can participate in their communities, while ensuring our athletes have the support they need to train, compete and succeed. Sport is one of our greatest nation builders, and these investments will help build a stronger Canada, inspire the next generation and leave a lasting legacy for everyone.”

—The Honourable Adam van Koeverden, Secretary of State (Sport)

“Sport has the power to bring people together, build confidence and create a sense of belonging in every community across Canada. Our government is making meaningful investments to expand access to sport so that more Canadians—especially youth and newcomers—can participate, connect and thrive. These investments will help create stronger, safer and more inclusive communities across the country, while supporting the athletes who inspire us all. This is how we continue to build Canada Strong for All.”

— The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship

Quick facts

• The Spring Economic Update 2026 proposes to provide $755 million over five years, starting in 2026–27, and $118 million ongoing, to Canadian Heritage to support Canada's sport system to: Host and compete with the best: $50 million over five years to bring more world-class sporting events to Canada. Funding will be tied to legacy-building projects that deliver lasting benefits well beyond the events themselves. Facilities built or upgraded for major events will continue to serve communities, support grassroots participation and strengthen local sport systems for years to come. Support our athletes and the next generation in performing at the highest levels: $45 million over five years and $8 million ongoing to help our athletes train, compete and perform, including support for better mental health and funding that will be linked to robust safe sport measures and frameworks. These actions will strengthen the sport system and respond to some of the findings of the Final Report of the Future of Sport in Canada Commission while the government continues to consider all of its recommendations. Get more Canadians involved in sport: $660 million over five years and $110 million ongoing for sport organizations, increasing funding that has remained largely unchanged since 2005, so that they can invest in a strong and safe sport system and grow participation among children and youth nationwide. We want sport organizations to work with private sector partners who share the goal of getting more Canadians involved in sport. We also expect sport organizations to make changes to their programming to invest in sport at all levels.

Related products

• Spring Economic Update 2026: Canada Strong for All

• Spring Economic Update 2026: Key Measures

• Spring Economic Update 2026: Address by the Minister of Finance and National Revenue

Contacts

Contacts for media only

Media Relations Canadian Heritage media@pch.gc.ca

Page details
items.bodyZh:
来源：加拿大移民、难民和公民部

新闻稿

在2025年预算案中，加拿大政府制定了建设加拿大强大的计划。自那以来，我们迅速采取行动建设主要基础设施、住房和产业，促进加拿大经济增长并创造持久繁荣；赋予加拿大人更好的职业前景和更经济的生活；保护我们的社区、边境和生活方式。

2026年5月2日——哈利法克斯——在2025年预算案中，加拿大政府制定了建设加拿大强大的计划。自那以来，我们迅速采取行动建设主要基础设施、住房和产业，促进加拿大经济增长并创造持久繁荣；赋予加拿大人更好的职业前景和更经济的生活；保护我们的社区、边境和生活方式。

我们在支持关键国家优先事项并保持投资聚焦于成果的同时，为加拿大人提供了具体的成本节约。我们保持着强劲的财政状况，2026年春季经济更新显示未来财政年度的预计赤字下降，我们正在按计划实现财政目标。

2026年春季经济更新《建设加拿大强大 惠及全民》(Spring Economic Update 2026: Canada Strong for All)计划中的下一步。它就加拿大经济的强势提供了清晰的最新信息，给加拿大人对我们的计划充满信心。它提供了有针对性的救济，以降低生活成本、支持工人，并加快住房和主要基础设施建设。它还在加强加拿大竞争力和经济增长的同时，对全国强大、安全的社区进行投资。

今日，荣誉部长Lena Metlege Diab（移民、难民和公民部部长）强调了2026年春季经济更新中体育投资的关键内容，以建设更强大、更安全的社区。

加拿大政府投资7.55亿加元以扩大体育参与机会，为加拿大人创造在其社区中建立有意义关系的机会，充分利用现有和新的基础设施，并支持加拿大世界级运动员，他们激励我们为国家的成就感到骄傲和团结。

作为该投资的一部分，五月花冰壶俱乐部(Mayflower Curling Club)将获得400万加元，用于支持一个新的、现代化的、无障碍设施的建设。该项目将帮助推动该运动的发展，消除参与障碍，并创建一个包容性的场所，让各种背景和能力的加拿大人都可以参与并保持活跃。

加拿大新政府正在将我们的经济从依赖转变为韧性。2026年春季经济更新确保所有加拿大人都可以参与建设加拿大强大，并分享其成功。其他关键措施包括：

• 加拿大强劲基金(Canada Strong Fund)——加拿大首个国家主权财富基金。这将投资于关键的战略性加拿大项目和公司。虽然加拿大人将通过这些国家建设项目受益于就业、经济增长和更大的安全保障，但政府致力于确保加拿大人也在这些项目中拥有权益。这就是加拿大强劲基金为何具有独特且重要特征的原因——其新的零售投资产品。这使加拿大人能够在我们一起建设加拿大强大的过程中获得财务回报。

• 加拿大强劲队伍(Team Canada Strong)——一项全国范围的新举措，目标是到2030-31年招募、培训和雇用80,000至100,000名新的熟练技工。该举措为加拿大人创造新机会，并吸引建设更多住房和大型项目所需的工人，以加快速度和规模。

• 建设更强大的社区(Building Stronger Communities)——通过使社区更安全、更互联和更具韧性。我们在建设更多住房、严厉打击犯罪和欺诈，以及为必要基础设施进行资金投入，包括维持沿海社区和当地就业的小型船舶港口。我们也在投资建设更健康、更安全和更强大的土著社区。

加拿大新政府正在建设一个不仅强大而且良好的加拿大；不仅繁荣而且公平的加拿大；不仅在某些时候为少数人，而是在所有时候为所有人服务的加拿大。我们正在建设加拿大强大 惠及全民。

引用

"2026年春季经济更新建立在我们预算案的势头之上，将战略投资与持续的财政纪律相结合，继续建设加拿大强大 惠及全民——为今天提供繁荣，为明天加强我们的经济。在加拿大历史的这个关键时刻，我们正以实力、决心和雄心穿过不确定性的迷雾和全球逆风，建设一个强大的加拿大经济，由加拿大人建设，为加拿大人服务。"

——荣誉财政部长兼国家收入部部长François-Philippe Champagne

"这是对体育的一代投资，因为体育远不止竞争。它关乎归属感、身体活动、信心和机会。从操场到领奖台，我们正在扩大体育参与的机会，让更多加拿大人可以参与他们的社区，同时确保我们的运动员获得他们需要的支持来训练、竞争和成功。体育是我们最伟大的国家建设者之一，这些投资将帮助建设一个更强大的加拿大，激励下一代，并为所有人留下遗产。"

——荣誉体育国务秘书Adam van Koeverden

"体育有能力将人们聚集在一起，建立信心，并在加拿大各地的每个社区中创造归属感。我们政府正在进行有意义的投资以扩大体育的参与机会，使更多加拿大人——特别是青年和新移民——能够参与、连接和繁荣。这些投资将帮助在全国各地创建更强大、更安全和更具包容性的社区，同时支持激励我们所有人的运动员。这就是我们继续建设加拿大强大 惠及全民的方式。"

——荣誉移民、难民和公民部部长Lena Metlege Diab

快速事实

• 2026年春季经济更新提议向加拿大遗产部(Canadian Heritage)提供7.55亿加元，为期五年，自2026-27年开始，以及1.18亿加元作为持续性拨款，以支持加拿大体育系统：主办和与最优秀的对抗：未来五年投入5,000万加元，为加拿大带来更多世界级体育赛事。资金将与遗产建设项目挂钩，这些项目将提供超越赛事本身的持久收益。为主要赛事建造或升级的设施将继续服务社区，支持基层参与，并在未来数年加强当地体育系统。支持我们的运动员和下一代以最高水平表现：未来五年投入4,500万加元，以及800万加元作为持续性拨款，以帮助我们的运动员训练、竞争和表现，包括支持更好的心理健康和与强有力的安全运动措施和框架挂钩的资金。这些行动将加强体育系统，并回应《加拿大体育未来委员会最终报告》(Final Report of the Future of Sport in Canada Commission)的一些调查结果，同时政府继续审议其所有建议。让更多加拿大人参与体育：未来五年投入6.6亿加元，以及1.1亿加元作为持续性拨款，给体育组织，增加自2005年以来基本保持不变的资金，以便他们可以投资于一个强大和安全的体育系统，并在全国范围内扩大儿童和青少年参与。我们希望体育组织与共享让更多加拿大人参与体育目标的私营部门合作伙伴合作。我们也期待体育组织对其编程进行改变，以在所有级别投资于体育。

相关产品

• 2026年春季经济更新：加拿大强大 惠及全民

• 2026年春季经济更新：关键措施

• 2026年春季经济更新：财政部长兼国家收入部部长的演讲

联系方式

仅限媒体的联系方式

加拿大遗产部媒体关系 media@pch.gc.ca

items.summaryZh: 加拿大政府在2026年春季经济更新中宣布向体育投资7.55亿加元，用于扩大体育项目的参与机会、支持运动员发展和建设更强大、更安全的社区。移民、难民和公民部部长Lena Metlege Diab强调了这些投资对包括新移民在内的全加拿大社区的积极影响。

items.title: Minister Diab to highlight Spring Economic Update 2026 sport investments in Timberlea
items.bodyEn:
From: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

Media advisory

Timberlea, Nova Scotia, May 1, 2026—The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship, will hold a press conference in Timberlea to highlight recent sport investments from the Spring Economic Update 2026: Canada Strong for All, focused on building stronger and safer communities.

Timberlea, Nova Scotia, May 1, 2026 —The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship, will hold a press conference in Timberlea to highlight recent sport investments from the Spring Economic Update 2026: Canada Strong for All, focused on building stronger and safer communities.

Please note that all details are subject to change.

The details are as follows:

Date:

Saturday, May 2, 2026

Time:

9:00 a.m. AT

Notes for media:

• Media are asked to register in advance for this event by sharing their name, title, email address and outlet with IRCC.Info-Info.IRCC@cic.gc.ca by Friday, May 1, at 7:00 p.m. AT . Please include “RSVP for May 2 press conference” in the subject line of the email. Details on how to attend will be provided afterward.

• Media attending the event in person are asked to arrive no later than 8:45 a.m. AT.

For more information (media only):

Taous Ait Senior Communications Advisor and Press Secretary Office of the Minister Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Taous.Ait@cic.gc.ca

Media Relations People and Communications Sector Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada media@cic.gc.ca

Page details
items.bodyZh:
来源：加拿大移民、难民和公民部

媒体通告

2026年5月1日，新斯科舍省Timberlea——加拿大移民、难民和公民部部长荣誉阁下Lena Metlege Diab将在Timberlea举办新闻发布会，重点介绍《2026年春季经济更新：加拿大强而有力》(Spring Economic Update 2026: Canada Strong for All)中的最近体育投资，重点关注建设更加强大和安全的社区。

请注意，所有细节均如有变更。

具体信息如下：

时间：

2026年5月2日（星期六）

时刻：

上午9:00 AT（大西洋时间）

媒体事项：

• 媒体被要求提前注册此次活动，请将姓名、职务、电子邮箱地址和所属媒体机构发送至IRCC.Info-Info.IRCC@cic.gc.ca，截止时间为5月1日（星期五）晚上7:00 AT。请在邮件主题行中注明"RSVP for May 2 press conference"（5月2日新闻发布会回复确认）。具体参加方式将在之后提供。

• 现场出席此次活动的媒体请不迟于上午8:45 AT到达。

更多信息（仅限媒体）：

Taous Ait 资深传播顾问兼新闻秘书 加拿大移民、难民和公民部部长办公室 Taous.Ait@cic.gc.ca

items.title: Update: 2025 Temporary Public Policy to Facilitate Work Permits for Prospective Provincial Nominee Program Candidates
items.bodyEn:
The Manitoba Provincial Nominee Program (MPNP) will conduct targeted Expression of Interest (EOI) draws to invite individuals who were issued Support Letters under the Temporary Public Policy to Facilitate Work Permits for Prospective Provincial Nominee Program Candidates, and who have successfully obtained a work permit under this public policy, to apply for provincial nomination.

All individuals who were issued a Support Letter will receive an Invitation to Apply (ITA), beginning with those approved between April 22 and June 30, 2025. ITAs will be sent to the email address provided in candidates’ Support Letter applications.

Support Letter recipients approved after June 30, 2025 can continue to monitor https://immigratemanitoba.com for updates on the next round of ITAs.

Please do not contact the MPNP to request an ITA if your Support Letter was approved after June 30, 2025.

For eligibility requirements, please click here to link to a FAQ page.

If you have any questions, please complete a Contact Us Webform, available online at https://immigratemanitoba.com/contact/ . From the drop-down list, select “I am a support letter recipient under the 2024 or 2025 Temporary Public Policy to Facilitate Work Permits for Prospective Provincial Nominee Program Candidates and have questions.” Inquiries about, or requests for ITAs for candidates who were approved after June 30 will not be responded to.
items.bodyZh:
曼尼托巴省提名计划(Manitoba Provincial Nominee Program, MPNP)将开展有针对性的意向表达(Expression of Interest, EOI)抽签，邀请在《前景省提名计划候选人工作许可便利临时公共政策》(Temporary Public Policy to Facilitate Work Permits for Prospective Provincial Nominee Program Candidates)下获得支持信且已成功获得工作许可的个人提出省提名申请。

所有获得支持信的个人都将收到申请邀请(Invitation to Apply, ITA)，首先从2025年4月22日至6月30日期间获批的人员开始。申请邀请将发送至候选人在支持信申请中提供的电子邮箱地址。

在2025年6月30日之后获批的支持信收件人可继续查看https://immigratemanitoba.com 网站以了解下一轮申请邀请的最新信息。

如果您的支持信是在2025年6月30日之后获批，请勿联系MPNP申请ITA。

关于资格要求，请点击此处链接到常见问题页面。

如您有任何疑问，请通过https://immigratemanitoba.com/contact/ 网址提交"联系我们"网络表单。从下拉列表中选择"我是2024年或2025年《前景省提名计划候选人工作许可便利临时公共政策》下的支持信收件人，有相关问题"。对于2025年6月30日之后获批的候选人的相关咨询或ITA申请将不予回复。
items.summaryZh: 曼尼托巴省提名计划(MPNP)将进行有针对性的意向表达(EOI)抽签，邀请曾获得临时公共政策支持信且已成功获得工作许可的人士申请省提名。首批获批人员(2025年4月22日至6月30日期间获批)将全部收到申请邀请(ITA)，后续获批者可持续关注官方网站获取后续ITA信息。

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited in the Greater Toronto Area ( GTA )
items.bodyEn:
On April 30, 2026, we issued 1063 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker or International Student streams. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in the Greater Toronto Area ( GTA ).

The Greater Toronto Area ( GTA ) includes the following Census Divisions: Durham, Halton, Peel, Toronto, and York.

Candidates with a score of 57 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 00012 – Senior managers – financial, communications and other business services

• NOC 00013 – Senior managers – health, education, social and community services and membership organizations

• NOC 10011 – Human resources managers

• NOC 10019 – Other administrative services managers

• NOC 12100 – Executive assistants

• NOC 12102 – Procurement and purchasing agents and officers

• NOC 21231 – Software engineers and designers

• NOC 21232 – Software developers and programmers

• NOC 21233 – Web designers

• NOC 21234 – Web developers and programmers

• NOC 21311 – Computer engineers (except software engineers and designers)

• NOC 21331 – Geological engineers

• NOC 22222 – Information systems testing technicians

• NOC 30010 – Managers in health care

Candidates with a score of 81 and above received an invitation under the International Student stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 10019 – Other administrative services managers

• NOC 10021 – Banking, credit and other investment managers

• NOC 32101 – Licensed practical nurses

• NOC 32121 – Medical radiation technologists

• NOC 32123 – Cardiology technologists and electrophysiological diagnostic technologists

• NOC 32124 – Pharmacy technicians

• NOC 32129 – Other medical technologists and technicians

• NOC 33100 – Dental assistants and dental laboratory assistants

• NOC 33102 – Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates

• NOC 33103 – Pharmacy technical assistants and pharmacy assistants

• NOC 33109 – Other assisting occupations in support of health services

• NOC 70010 – Construction managers

• NOC 70011 – Home building and renovation managers

• NOC 72010 – Contractors and supervisors, machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting trades and related occupations

• NOC 72205 – Telecommunications equipment installation and cable television service technicians

• NOC 73201 – General maintenance workers and building superintendents

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by April 28, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Your employer must review the employer guide and submit their application within 14 calendar days.

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX. You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.

items.title: Nominee Program Priorities in Nova Scotia
items.bodyEn:
April 27, 2026 Nominee Program Priorities in Nova Scotia

Nova Scotia’s nominee programs are focused on addressing the province’s most critical labour‑market needs while managing limited nomination spaces, with an emphasis on candidates who demonstrate a genuine intention to live, work, and settle long‑term in the province.

Current priority focus

Healthcare and skilled trades occupations

These occupations are a top priority for nomination at TEER levels 0–4 only and may be considered for both:

• International applicants, and

• Temporary residents already living and working in Nova Scotia

TEER 5 occupations are not being prioritized.

Other priority occupations

In addition to healthcare and skilled trades, Nova Scotia may consider nominations for temporary residents who are already living and working in the province in the following occupational groups, where they align with program criteria and labour‑market needs:

• NOC 2 – Natural and Applied Sciences and Related Occupations

• NOC 4 – Education, Law and Social, Community and Government Services

• NOC 8 – Natural Resources, Agriculture and Related Production Occupations

• NOC 9 – Manufacturing and Utilities

Consideration under these occupational groups is limited to TEER levels 0–4 only.

TEER 5 occupations are not being prioritized at this time.

Limited consideration for remaining occupations

For occupations outside the priority groups, nominations are currently limited to:

• TEER 0, 1, and 2 only, and

• Temporary residents already living and working in Nova Scotia

TEER 5 occupations are not being prioritized in any category at this time.

Program priorities may shift as labour‑market needs and nomination allocations change. Applicants are encouraged to monitor program updates regularly.
items.bodyZh:
2026年4月27日 新斯科舍省提名项目优先级

新斯科舍省的提名项目致力于解决该省最紧迫的劳动力市场需求，同时管理有限的提名名额，特别强调那些表现出真正意愿在该省长期居住、工作和定居的候选人。

当前优先重点

医疗卫生和技工职业

这些职业是TEER等级0–4的提名最高优先级，可能会被考虑用于：

• 国际申请人，以及

• 已在新斯科舍省居住和工作的临时居民

TEER 5职业不被优先考虑。

其他优先职业

除医疗卫生和技工外，对于已在该省居住和工作且符合项目标准和劳动力市场需求的临时居民，新斯科舍省可能会考虑以下职业组的提名：

• NOC 2 – 自然科学和应用科学及相关职业

• NOC 4 – 教育、法律及社会、社区和政府服务

• NOC 8 – 自然资源、农业及相关生产职业

• NOC 9 – 制造业和公用事业

在这些职业组下的考虑仅限于TEER等级0–4。

TEER 5职业目前不被优先考虑。

对其他剩余职业的有限考虑

对于不在优先组别内的职业，提名目前仅限于：

• TEER 0、1和2，以及

• 已在新斯科舍省居住和工作的临时居民

TEER 5职业在任何类别中目前都不被优先考虑。

随着劳动力市场需求和提名配额的变化，项目优先级可能会调整。建议申请人定期关注项目更新。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大新斯科舍省调整了移民提名项目的优先级，重点关注医疗卫生和技工等紧缺行业，同时限制名额分配。该政策主要影响有意在该省长期定居工作的国际申请人和临时居民。

items.title: Expression of Interest (EOI) Validity Period and Transition Measures
items.bodyEn:
April 27, 2026

Effective May 1, 2026

Effective May 1, 2026, Nova Scotia will introduce a 12‑month validity period for Expressions of Interest (EOIs) as part of improved inventory management and program efficiency measures. To support a smooth transition, a phased approach will apply to EOIs currently in the system.

Why this change is being introduced

These measures ensure that EOIs in the system are current, accurate, and aligned with program and labour‑market needs, while maintaining a sufficient and stable inventory to support nominations and processing in 2026 .

Applicants are encouraged to monitor program updates and submit a new EOI if their circumstances change or if their EOI expires.

How the transition will work

EOIs submitted before May 1, 2024

• EOIs submitted two or more years ago (before May 1, 2024) will be closed effective May 1, 2026.

• Closing an EOI is not a refusal.

• Affected candidates may submit a new EOI at any time, provided they meet eligibility requirements.

EOIs submitted between May 1, 2024 and April 30, 2026

• EOIs submitted during this period will remain active.

• If not selected, these EOIs will expire on April 30, 2027.

• No action is required from applicants at this time unless their circumstances change.

EOIs submitted on or after May 1, 2026

• EOIs submitted on or after May 1, 2026 will be subject to a 12‑month validity period from the date of submission.

• EOIs not selected within 12 months will expire and be removed from the pool.

• Expired EOIs may be resubmitted if the applicant continues to meet eligibility criteria.

What an EOI expiry means

• Expiry or closure of an EOI does not mean an application has been refused.

• EOIs are expressions of interest, not applications for nomination.

• Applicants may submit a new EOI at any time, as long as they meet program requirements.
items.bodyZh:
**标题：意向书（EOI）有效期和过渡措施**

2026年4月27日

自2026年5月1日起生效

自2026年5月1日起，新斯科舍省将在改进库存管理和项目效率措施的框架下，对意向书(Expression of Interest, EOI)引入12个月的有效期。为了支持平稳过渡，对系统中现存的EOI将采用分阶段方式。

**为什么引入这项变化**

这些措施确保系统中的EOI是最新的、准确的，并与项目和劳动力市场需求保持一致，同时保持足够且稳定的库存以支持2026年的提名和处理工作。

申请人被鼓励关注项目更新，如果其情况发生变化或其EOI到期，应提交新的EOI。

**过渡方式**

2024年5月1日前提交的EOI

• 两年或以上前（2024年5月1日前）提交的EOI将在2026年5月1日起关闭。

• 关闭EOI不等同于申请被拒。

• 受影响的申请人可以在任何时间提交新的EOI，但需满足资格要求。

2024年5月1日至2026年4月30日期间提交的EOI

• 在此期间提交的EOI将保持有效。

• 如未被选中，这些EOI将在2027年4月30日到期。

• 申请人暂无需采取行动，除非其情况发生变化。

2026年5月1日或之后提交的EOI

• 2026年5月1日或之后提交的EOI将受12个月有效期限制，有效期自提交之日起计算。

• 未在12个月内被选中的EOI将到期并从库池中移除。

• 如申请人继续满足资格要求，到期的EOI可以重新提交。

**EOI到期或关闭意味着什么**

• EOI的到期或关闭不意味着申请被拒。

• EOI是意向书，而非提名申请。

• 申请人可以在任何时间提交新的EOI，只要符合项目要求。
items.summaryZh: 新斯科舍省自2026年5月1日起实施意向书(EOI)12个月有效期制度，对系统中已有的EOI采用分阶段过渡措施：2024年5月前提交的EOI将被关闭，2024年5月至2026年4月提交的EOI保持有效至2027年4月，2026年5月后提交的EOI遵循新的12个月有效期规则。这项改革旨在确保库存中的EOI信息准确且与劳动力市场需求相符，同时维持充足稳定的申请人库。

items.title: BC Provincial Nominee Program Priorities and Program Updates
items.bodyEn:
BC Provincial Nominee Program Priorities and Program Updates

• The Entry Level and Semi Skilled (ELSS) stream is officially closed. The last invitations for this stream were issued on December 10, 2024. ELSS will soon be removed from the registration system and Program Guide. Individuals who were planning to apply under the ELSS stream may wish to explore alternative pathways to immigrate to BC.

• The BC PNP will not be launching new student streams. International student graduates may wish to explore alternative pathways to immigrate to B.C. through existing BC PNP streams. Completion of studies in B.C./Canada will continue to receive additional registration points under the BC PNP.

• The final draw for BC PNP priority technology occupations occurred on December 3, 2024. Going forward, the BC PNP will continue to issue targeted invitations for individuals creating high economic impact in B.C. to capture top talent across all sectors, including technology. All occupations on the previous technology occupations list remain eligible for the BC PNP.

• The list of priority occupations related to healthcare and construction has been updated, and the program will be expanding the list of ineligible occupations and employers to focus nominations on priorities, strengthen program integrity, and ensure that British Columbians have opportunities in these roles.

• 3,887 healthcare professionals , including 475 doctors and 1,228 nurses and nurse practitioners

• 2,957 licensed childcare workers

• 826 construction and trades workers after targeting select certified trades

• 26 veterinarians and 116 veterinary technologists , many working outside Metro Vancouver

• Over 38% of Skills Immigration nominees are working in regional communities, supporting population and economic growth across B.C.
items.bodyZh:
**BC省提名计划优先事项和项目更新**

• Entry Level and Semi Skilled (ELSS)流（入门级和半技能流）正式关闭。该流最后的邀请在2024年12月10日发出。ELSS将很快从注册系统和项目指南中移除。计划在ELSS流下申请的个人可能希望探索其他途径移民到BC省。

• BC PNP将不会推出新的学生流。国际学生毕业生可能希望通过现有的BC PNP流探索其他途径移民到BC省。在BC省/加拿大完成学业将继续在BC PNP下获得额外的注册分数。

• BC PNP优先技术职业的最后一轮抽签发生在2024年12月3日。今后，BC PNP将继续为在BC省创造高经济影响的个人发出有针对性的邀请，以吸引各部门（包括技术领域）的顶尖人才。之前技术职业列表上的所有职业仍然符合BC PNP的资格。

• 与医疗卫生和建筑相关的优先职业列表已更新，该计划将扩大不符合资格的职业和雇主名单，以专注于优先事项，加强项目完整性，并确保英属哥伦比亚省民众在这些职位中拥有机会。

• 3,887名医疗卫生专业人员，包括475名医生和1,228名护士及执业护士

• 2,957名持证托儿所工作者

• 826名建筑和行业工人（在针对特定认证行业进行筛选后）

• 26名兽医和116名兽医技术人员，其中许多在温哥华都会区外工作

• 超过38%的技能移民(Skills Immigration)被提名者在区域社区工作，支持BC省各地的人口和经济增长。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大BC省提名计划调整政策：关闭ELSS低技能移民流、停止新学生流、重点转向医疗卫生、建筑和高经济贡献人才，并扩大不符合资格的职业和雇主名单以加强项目完整性。

Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in Central Ontario (excluding GTA ).

Central Ontario (excluding GTA ) includes the following Census Divisions: Dufferin, Grey, Simcoe, Waterloo, and Wellington.

• NOC 94111 – 塑料加工机器操作员

• NOC 94124 – 木工机器操作员

• NOC 94132 – 工业缝纫机操作员

• NOC 94201 – 电子产品组装员、制造商、检验员和测试员

• NOC 94211 – 其他木制品组装员和检验员

• NOC 95104 – 橡胶和塑料制品制造劳动者

符合条件的个人档案必须在2026年4月21日晚上11:59之前创建并经过认证。

申请方式:

• 查阅雇主工作邀请流页面,确定您是否符合要求,并确保您拥有必需文件。

• 您的雇主必须查阅雇主指南,并在14个日历日内提交申请。

• 登录OINP电子申报门户(OINP e-Filing Portal),点击前缀为JOXX的新创建文件号。您必须在我们发出邀请之日起17个日历日内提交申请和付款。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大安大略省提名计划(OINP)于2026年4月23日向428名候选人发出邀请,他们可能符合雇主工作邀请流(Employer Job Offer streams)的资格,包括外国工人、国际学生或紧缺技能三个分流。申请人必须目前在加拿大持有有效工作或学习许可证,并在中部安大略地区(除大多伦多地区外)拥有工作邀请。本轮邀请涉及多个NOC(国家职业分类)代码的特定职业,不同分流对应不同的最低评分要求。

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited in Southwestern Ontario
items.bodyEn:
On April 23, 2026, we issued 886 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker , nternational Student or In-Demand Skills streams . Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in Southwestern Ontario.

Southwestern Ontario includes the following Census Divisions: Brant, Bruce, Chatham-Kent, Elgin, Essex, Haldimand-Norfolk, Hamilton, Hamilton-Wentworth, Huron, Lambton, Middlesex, Niagara, Oxford, and Perth.

items.summaryZh: 加拿大移民局于2026年4月23日向886名符合雇主工作邀请(Employer Job Offer)三个流类的候选人发出了邀请。这些候选人必须现居加拿大持有效工作或学习许可证,且在西南安大略地区拥有工作邀请。本次邀请涵盖外国工人流、国际学生流和紧缺技能流三个类别,根据流类不同,所需分数要求从34分到84分不等。

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited in Northern Ontario
items.bodyEn:
On April 23, 2026, we issued 303 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker , International Student or In-Demand Skills streams. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in Northern Ontario.

Northern Ontario includes the following Census Divisions: Algoma, Cochrane, Greater Sudbury, Haliburton, Kenora, Manitoulin, Muskoka, Nipissing, Parry Sound, Rainy River, Sudbury, Thunder Bay, and Timiskaming.

Candidates with a score of 60 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 00015 – Senior managers – construction, transportation, production and utilities

• NOC 10010 – Financial managers

• NOC 11100 – Financial auditors and accountants

• NOC 11109 – Other financial officers

• NOC 11202 – Professional occupations in advertising, marketing and public relations

• NOC 12200 – Accounting technicians and bookkeepers

• NOC 12203 – Assessors, valuators and appraisers

• NOC 13102 – Payroll administrators

• NOC 21102 – Geoscientists and oceanographers

• NOC 21223 – Database analysts and data administrators

• NOC 21231 – Software engineers and designers

• NOC 21232 – Software developers and programmers

items.bodyZh:

北安省包括以下普查分区：阿尔戈玛（Algoma）、科克伦（Cochrane）、大萨德伯里（Greater Sudbury）、哈利伯顿（Haliburton）、凯诺拉（Kenora）、曼尼图林（Manitoulin）、麝香鹿（Muskoka）、尼皮辛（Nipissing）、帕里桑德（Parry Sound）、雨河（Rainy River）、萨德伯里（Sudbury）、雷湾（Thunder Bay）和蒂米斯卡明（Timiskaming）。

items.summaryZh: 加拿大安省提名计划（OINP）于2026年4月23日向303名候选人发放了雇主工作邀请（Employer Job Offer）流的申请邀请，涵盖外国工人、国际学生和紧缺技能三个类别。申请人必须持有有效工签或学签、目前在加拿大居住，且在北安省拥有工作机会。

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited in Eastern Ontario
items.bodyEn:
On April 23, 2026, we issued 539 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker , International Student or In-Demand Skills streams. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in Eastern Ontario.

Eastern Ontario includes the following Census Divisions: Frontenac, Hastings, Kawartha Lakes, Lanark, Leeds and Grenville, Lennox and Addington, Northumberland, Ottawa, Ottawa-Carleton, Peterborough, Prescott and Russell, Prince Edward, Renfrew, and Stormont, Dundas and Glengarry.

Candidates with a score of 63 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

items.title: Expression of Interest Draw #269
items.bodyEn:
Skilled Worker in Manitoba

Occupation-specific selection

Profiles declaring current employment in Manitoba in the broad occupational category listed below were considered.

• Number of Letters of Advice to Apply issued: 192

Occupations under the following broad occupational category were considered in this draw:

• 3 – Health occupations

Skilled Worker Stream

Profiles submitted under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba pathway or the Skilled Worker Overseas pathway that declared being directly invited by the MPNP under a strategic recruitment initiative.

• Number of Letters of Advice to Apply issued: 116

The following numbers of Letters of Advice to Apply were issued to candidates declaring receipt of an Invitation to Apply (ITA) under the strategic recruitment initiatives listed below:

• Employer Services: 61

• Ethnocultural Communities: 14

• Francophone Community: 15

• Regional Communities: 14

• Temporary Public Policy to Facilitate Work Permits for Prospective Provincial Nominee Program Candidates (TPP): 12

Of the 308 Letters of Advice to Apply issued in this draw, 105 were issued to candidates who declared a valid Express Entry profile number and job seeker validation code.

Notes

• Expressions of Interest from Skilled Worker Stream candidates have only been considered if they were directly invited by the MPNP under a strategic recruitment initiative or through the Temporary Public Policy to Facilitate Work Permits for Prospective Provincial Nominee Program Candidates.

• If you have submitted an EOI profile which meets the above listed selection criteria but you have not received a Letter of Advice to Apply, this could be because you have indicated that you have taken an approved third-party language test to demonstrate your language ability in English/French, but have not provided a valid test number in your EOI or your language test is no longer valid; or you have indicated that you have received an Invitation to Apply to the MPNP under a strategic recruitment initiative, but did not provide a valid invitation number.

• Please note that for each question in the EOI, help text is available, providing detailed information on the information you should enter. If one of these scenarios applies to you, you may wish to update your EOI with the correct information in order to be considered for the next draw from the EOI pool.

• The Skilled Worker in Manitoba Occupation-specific selection considered candidates who declared meeting all Skilled Worker in Manitoba criteria, and ongoing Manitoba employment in the identified broad occupational category. Candidates with profiles submitted under the International Education Stream (IES) were not considered in this EOI draw.
items.bodyZh:
**意向书抽签第269期**

**曼尼托巴省技术工人**

**职业特定选择**

考虑了在下列列出的宽泛职业类别中声称目前在曼尼托巴省有工作的档案。

• 签发的申请建议信数量：192份

本次抽签考虑了以下宽泛职业类别中的职业：

• 3 – 卫生职业

**技术工人流**

在"曼尼托巴省技术工人"途径或"海外技术工人"途径下提交的档案，且声称被曼尼托巴省提名计划(MPNP)在战略招聘计划下直接邀请的申请人。

• 签发的申请建议信数量：116份

以下为根据下列战略招聘计划中声称收到申请邀请(ITA)的申请人所签发的申请建议信数量：

• 雇主服务(Employer Services)：61份

• 民族文化社区(Ethnocultural Communities)：14份

• 法语社区(Francophone Community)：15份

• 区域社区(Regional Communities)：14份

• 临时公共政策以便利为省提名计划候选人办理工作许可证(TPP)：12份

本次抽签中签发的308份申请建议信中，有105份签发给声称拥有有效快速入境(Express Entry)档案号码和求职者验证代码的申请人。

**说明**

• 来自技术工人流的意向书只有在被曼尼托巴省提名计划在战略招聘计划下直接邀请，或通过临时公共政策以便利为省提名计划候选人办理工作许可证的情况下才会被考虑。

• 如您提交的意向书档案符合上述列出的选择标准，但未收到申请建议信，这可能是因为您表示已参加经批准的第三方语言考试以证明您的英语/法语语言能力，但未在意向书中提供有效的考试号码或您的语言考试已不再有效；或您表示已收到曼尼托巴省提名计划战略招聘计划下的申请邀请，但未提供有效的邀请号码。

• 请注意，对于意向书中的每个问题，都有可用的帮助文本，提供有关您应输入的信息的详细信息。如果您属于上述任何情况，您可能希望使用正确的信息更新您的意向书，以便在下一次从意向书池进行的抽签中被考虑。

• "曼尼托巴省技术工人"职业特定选择考虑了声称符合"曼尼托巴省技术工人"所有标准的申请人，以及在确定的宽泛职业类别中的持续曼尼托巴省工作。在本次意向书抽签中不考虑在国际教育流(IES)下提交的档案申请人。
items.summaryZh: 曼尼托巴省技术工人项目(MPNP)进行了第269次意向书(EOI)抽签，共发放308份申请建议信。本次抽签分两部分：职业特定选择针对在曼省卫生职业领域工作的申请人(192份)；技术工人流则优先考虑通过战略招聘计划直接获邀的申请人(116份)。

items.title: Masters and PhD Graduate streams: candidates invited
items.bodyEn:
On April 22, 2026, we issued 674 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify under the Masters Graduate stream and 244 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify under the PhD Graduate stream. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit.

Candidates with a score of 61 and above received an invitation under the Masters Graduate stream. Candidates with a score of 56 and above received an invitation under the PhD Graduate stream.

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by April 20, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Masters Graduate or PhD Graduate stream pages to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix NMAS or NPHD. You must submit your application and payment within 14 calendar days.

items.title: Minister Diab concludes Citizenship Week 2026 by welcoming new Canadians
items.bodyEn:
From: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada

News release

Citizenship Week is an opportunity to reflect on what it means to be Canadian, celebrate the people who choose to call Canada home and recognize the Indigenous Peoples who have lived on this land since time immemorial.

April 20, 2026 — Ottawa —Citizenship Week is an opportunity to reflect on what it means to be Canadian, celebrate the people who choose to call Canada home and recognize the Indigenous Peoples who have lived on this land since time immemorial.

The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship, marked the week by participating in ceremonies in Ottawa, Ontario, and in Lunenburg and Halifax, Nova Scotia, celebrating this important milestone alongside Canada’s newest citizens, their families and their communities.

From April 12 to 18, over 80 citizenship ceremonies were held across the country, welcoming approximately 6,000 new citizens from 40 countries as they took the oath of citizenship—the final step in their journey to becoming Canadian—and began a new chapter in their lives.

Many ceremonies this week included the participation of an Indigenous Elder or community member, who offered a land acknowledgement, shared knowledge and a message of welcome. An important part of becoming Canadian is understanding the history and rich cultures of Indigenous Peoples in Canada, and our shared responsibility to participate in the ongoing work of reconciliation.

Throughout Citizenship Week, the Minister spoke about the importance of belonging, civic participation and our collective role in building a strong and inclusive Canada. She met with new citizens and their families, acknowledging their contributions and the part they will play in shaping the country’s future.

The Minister also highlighted the importance of community engagement and active citizenship. During the ceremony in Halifax, the Minister presented a young member of the Girl Guides of Canada with a signed Girl Guides Pathfinders Building Belonging Certificate in recognition of the group’s commitment to community and the values that bring Canadians together.

Canadian citizenship is more than a legal status; it is a connection to one another and to the future we are building together.

Photos of the Minister’s participation in this week’s citizenship ceremonies are available for media.

Quotes

“It’s a privilege to welcome new citizens to Canada and to recognize the journeys that have brought them here. These moments reflect the values that define Canada—openness, inclusion and the opportunity for all to succeed and contribute. As we celebrate these milestones, I encourage Canadians to reflect on what it means to be part of our diverse Canadian family, and to look ahead with confidence and hope.”

– The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab, Minister of Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship

Quick facts

• Citizenship Week is held annually to recognize and celebrate the rights, responsibilities and shared values of Canadian citizenship. Over 356,000 people became Canadian citizens annually on average over the past 4 calendar years (2022–2025). Next year (2027) will mark the 80th anniversary of the Canadian Citizenship Act and the 40th anniversary of Citizenship Week. The first Canadian citizenship ceremony was held on January 3, 1947, at the Supreme Court of Canada. The citizenship ceremony is the final step in becoming a Canadian citizen. During the ceremony, participants take the oath of citizenship and accept the rights and responsibilities of citizenship.

Associated links

• Citizenship Week

• Celebrating Canadian citizenship: Unity, belonging and building our nation together

Contacts

Contacts for media only

Taous Ait Senior Communications Advisor and Press Secretary Office of the Minister Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada Taous.Ait@cic.gc.ca

Media Relations People and Communications Sector Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada 613-952-1650 media@cic.gc.ca

Page details
items.bodyZh:
来源：加拿大移民、难民和公民事务部

新闻稿

公民周是反思加拿大身份意义、庆祝选择将加拿大视为家园的人们，以及承认自古以来生活在这片土地上的原住民的机会。

2026年4月20日——渥太华——公民周是反思加拿大身份意义、庆祝选择将加拿大视为家园的人们，以及承认自古以来生活在这片土地上的原住民的机会。

尊敬的莱娜·梅特勒格·迪亚布(The Honourable Lena Metlege Diab)部长，移民、难民和公民事务部部长，通过在安大略省渥太华以及新斯科舍省卢嫩堡和哈利法克斯举行的仪式来纪念这一周，与加拿大最新的公民、他们的家庭和社区一起庆祝这一重要里程碑。

从4月12日至18日，全国举办了超过80场公民宣誓仪式，欢迎来自40个国家的大约6000名新公民宣誓入籍——这是他们成为加拿大人旅程中的最后一步——并在他们的生活中开启了新的篇章。

本周许多仪式中都有原住民长者或社区成员的参与，他们进行了土地致敬(land acknowledgement)、分享知识和欢迎信息。成为加拿大人的重要部分是了解加拿大原住民的历史和丰富文化，以及我们在参与持续和解工作中的共同责任。

在整个公民周期间，部长谈论了归属感、公民参与和我们在建设强大而包容的加拿大中的集体作用的重要性。她与新公民及其家人会面，承认他们的贡献以及他们在塑造国家未来中将发挥的作用。

部长还强调了社区参与和积极公民身份的重要性。在哈利法克斯的仪式中，部长向加拿大女童军(Girl Guides of Canada)的一名年轻成员颁发了经过签署的女童军探路者建设归属证书(Girl Guides Pathfinders Building Belonging Certificate)，以表彰该组织对社区的承诺以及将加拿大人聚集在一起的价值观。

加拿大公民身份不仅仅是一种法律地位；它是对彼此的联系和我们共同建设的未来的承诺。

部长本周公民宣誓仪式参与活动的照片可供媒体使用。

引言

"欢迎新公民来到加拿大是一种荣幸，认可他们走过的旅程。这些时刻反映了定义加拿大的价值观——开放、包容以及所有人成功和贡献的机会。当我们庆祝这些里程碑时，我鼓励加拿大人反思作为我们多元加拿大大家庭一部分意味着什么，并以信心和希望展望未来。"

——尊敬的莱娜·梅特勒格·迪亚布，移民、难民和公民事务部部长

快速事实

• 公民周每年举办一次，以认可和庆祝加拿大公民身份的权利、责任和共同价值观。在过去4个日历年(2022–2025)期间，平均每年有超过356000人成为加拿大公民。明年(2027年)将标志《加拿大公民法》(Canadian Citizenship Act)的第80周年纪念和公民周的第40周年纪念。第一场加拿大公民宣誓仪式于1947年1月3日在加拿大最高法院举行。公民宣誓仪式是成为加拿大公民的最后一步。在仪式中，参与者宣誓入籍并接受公民身份的权利和责任。

相关链接

• 公民周

• 庆祝加拿大公民身份：团结、归属和建设我们的国家

媒体联系方式

塔乌斯·艾特(Taous Ait)高级通讯顾问兼新闻秘书
移民、难民和公民事务部部长办公室

items.summaryZh: 加拿大移民部长迪亚布(Lena Metlege Diab)以欢迎新公民的方式为2026年公民周(Citizenship Week)画上句号。4月12日至18日期间，全国举办了超过80场公民宣誓仪式，约6000名来自40个国家的新公民完成了入籍最后一步。本次活动强调了归属感、公民参与和和解承诺的重要性。

items.title: Employer Job Offer In-Demand Skills stream: candidates invited in agriculture-related and other priority occupations
items.bodyEn:
On April 15, 2026, we issued 1334 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify for the Employer Job Offer: In-Demand Skills stream. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in agriculture-related or other priority occupations.

Candidates in agriculture-related occupations with a score of 35 and above received an invitation. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 84120 - Specialized livestock workers and farm machinery operators

• NOC 85100 - Livestock labourers

• NOC 85103 - Nursery and greenhouse labourers

• NOC 94140 - Process control and machine operators, food and beverage processing

• NOC 94141 - Industrial butchers and meat cutters, poultry preparers and related workers

• NOC 95106 - Labourers in food and beverage processing

Candidates in other priority occupations with a score of 36 and above received an invitation. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 14400 - Shippers and receivers

• NOC 14402 - Production logistics workers

• NOC 44101 - Home support workers, housekeepers and related occupations

• NOC 74203 - Other automotive mechanical installers and servicers

• NOC 74204 - Waterworks and gas maintenance workers

• NOC 74205 - Public works maintenance equipment operators and related workers

• NOC 75101 - Material handlers

• NOC 75110 - Construction trades helpers and labourers

• NOC 75119 - Other trades helpers and labourers

• NOC 75211 - Railway and motor transport labourers

• NOC 75212 - Public works and maintenance labourers

• NOC 94100 - Machine operators, mineral and metal processing

• NOC 94101 - Foundry workers

• NOC 94103 - Concrete, clay and stone forming operators

• NOC 94105 - Metalworking and forging machine operators

• NOC 94106 - Machining tool operators

• NOC 94107 - Other metal products machine operators

• NOC 94110 - Chemical plant machine operators

• NOC 94111 - Plastics processing machine operators

• NOC 94112 - Rubber processing machine operators and related workers

• NOC 94120 - Sawmill machine operators

• NOC 94124 - Woodworking machine operators

• NOC 94132 - Industrial sewing machine operators

• NOC 94200 - Motor vehicle assemblers, inspectors and testers

• NOC 94202 - Assemblers and inspectors, electrical appliance, apparatus and equipment manufacturing

• NOC 94203 - Assemblers, fabricators and inspectors, industrial electrical motors and transformers

• NOC 94204 - Mechanical assemblers and inspectors

• NOC 94212 - Plastic products assemblers, finishers and inspectors

• NOC 94213 - Industrial painters, coaters and metal finishing process operators

• NOC 94219 - Other products assemblers, finishers and inspectors

• NOC 95100 - Labourers in mineral and metal processing

• NOC 95101 - Labourers in metal fabrication

• NOC 95103 - Labourers in wood, pulp and paper processing

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by April 13, 2026, 11:59 p.m. To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer: In Demand Skills stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Your employer must review the employer guide and submit their application within 14 calendar days.

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX . You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.
items.bodyZh:

items.title: Saskatchewan's Labour Market Remains Strong with 15,000 Full Time Jobs Created in March
items.bodyEn:
Released on April 10, 2026

The latest labour force numbers from Statistics Canada's show that the labour market in Saskatchewan remains strong with 15,000 full time jobs added year-over-year. Saskatchewan's unemployment rate of five per cent is the lowest amongst provinces and well below the national average of 6.7 per cent. Saskatchewan's seasonally adjusted employment growth rate was also second among provinces (0.9 per cent), increasing by 5,800 from the previous month.

"March was a strong month for Saskatchewan's labour market," Immigration and Career Training Minister Eric Schmalz said. "Our government is protecting Saskatchewan's workforce through partnerships with industry to ensure that Saskatchewan people are prepared to meet the demand created by our strong and growing economy."

Saskatchewan's two biggest cities also saw year-over-year growth. Compared to March 2025, Regina's employment is up 7,800 jobs, an increase of 5.3 per cent, and Saskatoon's employment is up 5,200, an increase of 2.6 per cent.

Youth employment in Saskatchewan is up 3,600 compared to March 2025, an increase of 4.6 per cent. The youth unemployment rate is 9.8 per cent, the third lowest among provinces and well below the national rate of 13.8 per cent. Major year-over-year gains were reported for healthcare and social assistance, up 6,800, an increase of 6.8 per cent. Other services such as personal and repair services are up 4,800, an increase of 19.7 per cent, and finance, insurance and real estate and leasing are up 3,500, an increase of 12.7 per cent.

Saskatchewan continues to show economic strength in other areas. Year-over-year, Saskatchewan ranked third among the provinces for growth in the value of building permits, and month-over-month ranked second among the provinces for growth in urban housing starts.

This economic growth is backed by the Government of Saskatchewan's Building the Workforce for a Growing Economy: The Saskatchewan Labour Market Strategy , a roadmap to build the workforce needed to support Saskatchewan's strong and growing economy, and Securing the Next Decade of Growth: Saskatchewan's Investment Attraction Strategy , a plan to increase investment in the province and to further advancing Saskatchewan's Growth plan goal of $16 billion in private capital investment annually.

-30-
items.bodyZh:
萨省劳动力市场保持强劲，3月份创造15,000个全职工作

发布于2026年4月10日

根据加拿大统计局最新劳动力数据显示，萨省劳动力市场保持强劲，年同比增加15,000个全职工作。萨省失业率为5%，是各省中最低的，远低于全国平均水平6.7%。萨省经过季节调整的就业增长率也在各省中排名第二（0.9%），环比增加5,800人。

"3月对萨省劳动力市场来说是强劲的一个月，"移民和职业培训部长埃里克·施马尔茨说。"我们的政府正在通过与行业的合作来保护萨省劳动力，以确保萨省人民做好准备以满足我们强劲且不断增长经济所产生的需求。"

萨省两个最大城市也实现了年同比增长。与2025年3月相比，里贾纳的就业增加7,800个，增幅为5.3%，萨斯卡通的就业增加5,200个，增幅为2.6%。

与2025年3月相比，萨省青年就业增加3,600人，增幅为4.6%。青年失业率为9.8%，在各省中排名第三，远低于全国平均水平13.8%。医疗保健和社会援助领域报告了重大年同比增长，增加6,800个，增幅为6.8%。其他服务如个人和修理服务增加4,800个，增幅为19.7%，金融、保险、房地产和租赁增加3,500个，增幅为12.7%。

萨省在其他领域也继续显示经济实力。年同比而言，萨省在建筑许可证价值增长方面在各省中排名第三，月同比而言在城市住房开工增长方面在各省中排名第二。

这一经济增长得到了萨省政府《建设不断增长经济的劳动力：萨省劳动力市场战略》(Building the Workforce for a Growing Economy: The Saskatchewan Labour Market Strategy)的支持，该战略是建设支持萨省强劲增长经济所需劳动力的路线图，以及《确保未来十年增长：萨省投资吸引战略》(Securing the Next Decade of Growth: Saskatchewan's Investment Attraction Strategy)的支持，该计划旨在增加对该省的投资，并进一步推进萨省增长计划目标，即年度私人资本投资160亿元。

-30-
items.summaryZh: 萨省3月份创造了15,000个全职工作，失业率为5%，在全国各省中最低。萨省经济持续增长，两大城市就业机会显著增加，青年就业也表现强劲。

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited in healthcare and early childhood education occupations
items.bodyEn:
On April 8, 2026, we issued 1,635 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify for the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker or International Student streams who work in healthcare or early childhood education occupations. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit.

Candidates with a score of 20 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 31300 - Nursing co-ordinators and supervisors

• NOC 31301 - Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses

• NOC 31302 - Nurse practitioners

• NOC 32101 - Licensed practical nurses

• NOC 33102 - Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates

• NOC 42202 - Early childhood educators and assistants

Candidates with a score of 46 and above received an invitation under the International Student stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 31300 - Nursing co-ordinators and supervisors

• NOC 31301 - Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses

• NOC 32101 - Licensed practical nurses

• NOC 33102 - Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates

• NOC 42202 - Early childhood educators and assistants

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by April 6, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Your employer must review the employer guide and submit their application within 14 calendar days.

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX . You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.
items.bodyZh:

在外劳流下，评分达到20分及以上的候选人获得邀请。候选人必须拥有下列国家职业分类(NOC)代码之一的工作邀请：

• NOC 42202 - 幼儿教育者和助理

在国际学生流下，评分达到46分及以上的候选人获得邀请。候选人必须拥有下列国家职业分类(NOC)代码之一的工作邀请：

• NOC 31300 - 护理协调员和主管

• NOC 31301 - 注册护士和注册精神病护士

申请方式：

• 您的雇主必须查看雇主指南，并在14个日历日内提交申请。

items.summaryZh: 加拿大移民局2026年4月8日向符合雇主工作邀请流(Employer Job Offer streams)条件的1,635名候选人发出邀请，这些候选人从事医疗保健或幼儿教育工作，且必须持有有效的工作或学习许可证在加拿大居住。根据职业代码和评分要求，外劳流(Foreign Worker stream)申请人需达到20分及以上，国际学生流(International Student stream)申请人需达到46分及以上。

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited under the Regional Economic Development through Immigration ( REDI ) pilot
items.bodyEn:
On April 8, 2026, we issued 32 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify for the REDI pilot under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker , International Student or In-Demand Skills streams. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in Lanark or Leeds and Grenville.

Candidates with a score of 46 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 10029 - Other business services managers

• NOC 11101 - Financial and investment analysts

• NOC 33102 - Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates

• NOC 72422 - Electrical mechanics

• NOC 72423 - Motorcycle, all-terrain vehicle and other related mechanics

Candidates with a score of 57 and above received an invitation under the International Student stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 22114 - Landscape and horticulture technicians and specialists

• NOC 32101 - Licensed practical nurses

• NOC 32109 - Other technical occupations in therapy and assessment

• NOC 33102 - Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates

• NOC 42201 - Social and community service workers

• NOC 42202 - Early childhood educators and assistants

Candidates with a score of 33 and above received an invitation under the In-Demand Skills stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 44101 - Home support workers, housekeepers and related occupations

• NOC 94100 - Machine operators, mineral and metal processing

• NOC 94111 - Plastics processing machine operators

• NOC 95106 - Labourers in food and beverage processing

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by April 6, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Your employer must review the employer guide and submit their application within 14 calendar days.

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX. You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.
items.bodyZh:
2026年4月8日，我们向可能符合区域经济发展移民(Regional Economic Development through Immigration, REDI)试点资格的候选人发出了32份申请邀请，该试点涵盖雇主工作邀请(Employer Job Offer)流下的外国劳工(Foreign Worker)、国际学生(International Student)或紧缺技能(In-Demand Skills)分流。候选人必须目前在加拿大持有有效的工作或学习许可证，并在Lanark或Leeds and Grenville地区获得工作邀请。

获得分数46分及以上的候选人收到了外国劳工(Foreign Worker)分流的邀请。候选人必须获得以下国家职业分类(National Occupational Classification, NOC)代码之一的工作邀请：

• NOC 72423 - 摩托车、全地形车及其他相关机械师

获得分数57分及以上的候选人收到了国际学生(International Student)分流的邀请。候选人必须获得以下国家职业分类(National Occupational Classification, NOC)代码之一的工作邀请：

• NOC 22114 - 景观和园艺技术员及专家

• NOC 32101 - 执业护士

• NOC 32109 - 治疗和评估方面的其他技术职业

• NOC 33102 - 护理助理、医疗护理员和患者服务助理

• NOC 42201 - 社会和社区服务工作者

• NOC 42202 - 幼儿教育工作者及助理

获得分数33分及以上的候选人收到了紧缺技能(In-Demand Skills)分流的邀请。候选人必须获得以下国家职业分类(National Occupational Classification, NOC)代码之一的工作邀请：

• NOC 44101 - 家庭支持工作者、保洁员及相关职业

• NOC 94100 - 机器操作员、矿物和金属加工

• NOC 94111 - 塑料加工机器操作员

• NOC 95106 - 食品和饮料加工业劳动者

符合条件的档案必须在2026年4月6日晚11:59前创建并证实。

申请方式：

• 查看雇主工作邀请(Employer Job Offer)分流页面，以确定您是否符合要求，并确保您拥有必需的文件。

• 您的雇主必须查看雇主指南，并在14个日历日内提交申请。

• 登录安大略省提名计划电子申报系统(OINP e-Filing Portal)，点击新创建的以JOXX为前缀的文件号。您必须在收到邀请之日起17个日历日内提交申请和缴费。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大移民部于2026年4月8日向符合区域经济发展移民试点(REDI pilot)资格的候选人发出32份申请邀请,涉及雇主工作邀请流下的外国劳工、国际学生和紧缺技能三个分流,申请人需在加拿大持有有效工作或学习许可证,并在Lanark或Leeds and Grenville地区获得工作邀请。三个分流的分数线和符合条件的职业代码各有不同要求。

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX. You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.

items.title: Employer Job Offer Foreign Worker stream: Physicians invited
items.bodyEn:
On April 8, 2026, we issued 15 invitations to apply to physicians who may qualify for the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream .

Candidates with a score of 43 and above received an invitation. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 31100 - Specialists in clinical and laboratory medicine

• NOC 31101 - Specialists in surgery

• NOC 31102 - General practitioners and family physicians

Please refer to the January 5 program update for more details.

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by April 6, 2026, 11:59 p.m. To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Your employer must review the employer guide and submit their application within 14 calendar days.

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX. You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.
items.bodyZh:
在2026年4月8日,我们向可能符合「雇主工作邀请:外国劳工流」(Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream)条件的医生发出了15份申请邀请。

分数达到43分及以上的候选人获得邀请。候选人必须持有以下「国家职业分类」(National Occupational Classification, NOC)代码之一的工作邀请:

• NOC 31100 - 临床和实验室医学专家

• NOC 31101 - 外科专家

• NOC 31102 - 全科医生和家庭医生

请参阅1月5日的项目更新了解更多详情。

符合条件的申请档案必须在2026年4月6日晚上11:59之前创建并通过认证。申请步骤:

• 查阅「雇主工作邀请流」页面,以确定您是否符合要求,并确保您拥有必需的文件。

• 您的雇主必须查阅雇主指南,并在14个日历天内提交申请。

• 登录OINP电子申报门户网站,点击新创建的前缀为JOXX的档案号。您必须在收到邀请之日起17个日历天内提交您的申请和付款。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大安省于2026年4月8日向15名医生发出了「雇主工作邀请：外国劳工流」的申请邀请,分数达到43分及以上的申请人获邀;符合条件的医生必须持有三类医疗专业的工作邀请函,包括临床和实验室医学专家、外科专家以及全科医生和家庭医生。

items.title: Worker Expression of Interest fee
items.bodyEn:
Effective April 7, 2026, the Alberta Advantage Immigration Program (AAIP) is introducing a $135 fee to submit a Worker Expression of Interest (WEOI). All other AAIP service fees remain unchanged at this time.

Read the 2026 AAIP fee schedule for full details.
items.bodyZh:
**工作者意向表达费用**

自2026年4月7日起，阿尔伯塔省优才移民计划(Alberta Advantage Immigration Program, AAIP)推出135加元费用用于提交工作者意向表达(Worker Expression of Interest, WEOI)。AAIP的所有其他服务费用目前保持不变。

请查阅2026年AAIP费用表了解完整细节。
items.summaryZh: 阿尔伯塔省移民项目从2026年4月7日起，对提交工作者意向表达(WEOI)收取135加元费用。这一新收费政策将影响所有通过该途径申请的海外工作者。

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited in mining-related occupations
items.bodyEn:
On April 1, 2026, we issued 759 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify for the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker , International Student or In-Demand Skills streams who work in mining-related occupations. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit.

Candidates with a score of 56 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 21310 - Electrical and electronics engineers

• NOC 21330 - Mining engineers

items.title: Regina Hosts 2026 Skills Canada Saskatchewan Provincial Competition
items.bodyEn:
Released on March 27, 2026

Today nearly 300 high school and post-secondary students as well as apprentices are gathering in Regina to showcase their talents at the 2026 Skills Canada Saskatchewan Provincial Competition held at Campus Regina Public and Saskatchewan Polytechnic's Regina campus.

"Congratulations to everyone competing at this year's Skills Canada Saskatchewan Provincial Competition," Immigration and Career Training Minister Eric Schmalz said. "There is a bright future for the skilled trades and technology sectors in our province. We are thrilled so many young people are honing their skills in these high-demand career pathways."

Competitors will compete in more than 40 competition categories, including autobody repair, carpentry, cooking, hairstyling, graphic design, photography and welding. Medal winners may have the opportunity to join Team Saskatchewan at the Skills Canada National Competition May 28 and 29 in Toronto.

"I want to thank the sponsors, judges, volunteers and educators who make this competition possible," Skills Canada Saskatchewan Executive Director Krystal Nieckar said. "The time and money they invest in our provincial competition ensure it is successful and sustainable for years to come. We are so proud to host an event that allows young people to demonstrate their dedication and talent."

The public is welcome to watch the competitions at Saskatchewan Polytechnic's Regina campus and Campus Regina Public from 9 a.m. until 3 p.m.

A career fair featuring hands-on activities will take place at Campus Regina Public in the school gym while competitions are taking place, and visitors can attend this event as well.

"Skilled trade employers tell us they're recruiting," Saskatchewan Apprenticeship and Trade Certification Commission CEO Jeff Ritter said. "They are looking for people who like to solve problems and work with their hands. The competitors today have a head start already - they are demonstrating their commitment to being the best they can be in their chosen trades."

As of December 31, 2025, there are nearly 7,800 apprentices registered in Saskatchewan, the highest number in nearly a decade.

"Skilled trades are the backbone of our economy," Saskatchewan Polytechnic President and CEO Dr. Larry Rosia said. "With hundreds of thousands of tradespeople across Canada set to retire by 2028, the Skills Canada Saskatchewan Provincial Competition highlights the up-and-coming talent in these trade and technology fields while showcasing the career opportunities a polytechnic education can provide. I wish the very best of luck to all the competitors!"

Skills competitions are run globally and culminate in a WorldSkills competition held every two years. In 2026, the WorldSkills Competition will take place September 22-27 in Shanghai.

For more provincial competition details, visit: skillscanadasask.com .
