items.bodyZh:
**里贾纳举办2026年加拿大技能竞赛萨斯喀彻温省分赛**

发布日期：2026年3月27日

今天,近300名高中和高等教育学生以及学徒正在里贾纳聚集,在里贾纳公立校园和萨斯喀彻温理工学院里贾纳校区举办的2026年加拿大技能竞赛萨斯喀彻温省分赛(2026 Skills Canada Saskatchewan Provincial Competition)上展示他们的才能。

"祝贺所有参加今年加拿大技能竞赛萨斯喀彻温省分赛的竞争者,"移民与职业培训部长埃里克·施马尔茨(Eric Schmalz)表示。"我们省的技能交易和技术部门有光明的未来。我们非常高兴看到这么多年轻人正在这些高需求的职业发展道路上磨练技能。"

竞争者将在包括汽车维修、木工、烹饪、美发、平面设计、摄影和焊接在内的40多个竞赛类别中竞争。奖牌获得者可能有机会加入萨省队,参加5月28日和29日在多伦多举办的加拿大技能竞赛全国竞赛(Skills Canada National Competition)。

"我要感谢赞助商、评委、志愿者和教育工作者使这项竞赛成为可能,"加拿大技能竞赛萨斯喀彻温省执行主任克里斯特尔·尼克卡尔(Krystal Nieckar)表示。"他们在我们的省级竞赛中投入的时间和资金确保了它在未来许多年内的成功和可持续性。我们为举办一个能让年轻人展示他们在所选技能领域的奉献精神和才能的活动感到骄傲。"

公众欢迎在萨斯喀彻温理工学院里贾纳校区和里贾纳公立校园观看竞赛,时间从上午9点至下午3点。

在举办竞赛的同时,一个包含实际操作活动的职业招聘会将在里贾纳公立校园的学校体育馆举办,访客也可以参加此活动。

"熟练交易雇主告诉我们他们在招聘,"萨斯喀彻温学徒和交易认证委员会首席执行官杰夫·里特(Jeff Ritter)表示。"他们在寻找喜欢解决问题和用手工作的人。今天的竞争者已经领先一步——他们正在展示自己致力于在所选交易中做到最好的承诺。"

截至2025年12月31日,萨斯喀彻温省注册学徒近7800人,这是近十年来最高的数字。

"熟练交易是我们经济的支柱,"萨斯喀彻温理工学院院长兼首席执行官劳里·罗西亚博士(Dr. Larry Rosia)表示。"随着加拿大数十万技工将在2028年前退休,加拿大技能竞赛萨斯喀彻温省分赛突出了这些交易和技术领域中的新兴人才,同时展示了理工学院教育能提供的职业机会。我祝所有竞争者好运!"

技能竞赛全球运行,最终汇聚到每两年举办一次的WorldSkills(世界技能竞赛)。2026年,世界技能竞赛将于9月22日至27日在上海举办。

如需了解更多省级竞赛详情,请访问:skillscanadasask.com。

-30-
items.summaryZh: 萨斯喀彻温省里贾纳市近日举办2026年全省技能竞赛,吸引约300名高中、高等教育学生和学徒参赛,竞争包括汽车维修、木工、烹饪、美发、平面设计、摄影和焊接等40多个项目。获奖者有机会代表萨省参加5月底在多伦多举办的全国技能竞赛。

items.title: Governments of Canada and Saskatchewan Partner to Protect Tariff-Impacted Workers and Strengthen the Workforce
items.bodyEn:
Released on March 27, 2026

The global trade landscape is shifting rapidly, creating uncertainty and challenges for workers, industries and communities across Canada. In a rapidly changing world, Canada must focus on what it can control: building a stronger, more resilient nation. This calls for decisive action from governments at all levels to protect jobs, strengthen local economies and ensure workers can adapt to changing economic realities.

Workers whose jobs have been directly or indirectly impacted by global tariffs will receive support to help them adapt, retrain and succeed, as a result of a partnership agreement announced today by the Honourable Buckley Bélanger, Secretary of State (Rural Development) on behalf of the Honourable Patty Hajdu, Minister of Jobs and Families and Minister responsible for the Federal Economic Development Agency for Northern Ontario, alongside the Honourable Eric Schmalz, Saskatchewan’s Minister of Government Relations; Minister of Immigration and Career Training; Minister responsible for First Nations, Métis and Northern Affairs; and Minister responsible for the Provincial Capital Commission.

Specifically, $15.6 million over three years will be invested through the new Canada–Saskatchewan Workforce Tariff Response to support workers and employers in steel, softwood lumber, and other directly and indirectly tariff-affected sectors. It is anticipated this new funding will support up to 1,800 workers in Saskatchewan build new skills and seize emerging opportunities.

Supports will be delivered through the existing province-wide network of SaskJobs offices and training partners ensuring timely support for workers who may benefit from retraining or employment assistance as they transition into new opportunities, including:

• unemployed workers building skills for in-demand jobs;

• workers whose employers are participating in Employment Insurance (EI) Work-Sharing agreements, allowing them to upskill or retrain as these industries adapt; and,

• employed workers strengthening their skills and resilience within companies or supply chains directly affected by tariffs and global market shifts.

Implementation of the partnership agreement will leverage both existing and new or enhanced programs and services. Coordinating directly with impacted businesses to protect jobs will give tariff‑affected workers and those in EI Work‑Sharing agreements improved opportunities for upskilling or retraining, in a changing economic landscape.

This approach reflects a shared commitment by the governments of Canada and Saskatchewan to support Canadians through a period of significant economic adjustment, while building a strong, confident workforce—one where workers can navigate global uncertainty and industries can remain competitive in the global marketplace.

Quotes

“Canada’s workforce is strongest when employers and training partners work together. As global trade evolves, no worker will be left behind," Minister of Jobs and Families and Minister responsible for the Federal Economic Development Agency for Northern Ontario Patty Hajdu said. "Through the Workforce Tariff Response, that means providing the tools and training workers need to secure good jobs, continue building strong communities, and ensure Canada remains resilient in the face of global challenges.”

“Today’s partnership marks a significant step forward in our commitment to support workers impacted by global tariffs," Secretary of State (Rural Development) Buckley Belanger said. "By investing in retraining and skill development, we are empowering individuals to adapt to the changing economy and ensuring that they can thrive in new opportunities. Together with our provincial partners, we are fostering a resilient workforce that not only meets the challenges of today but also seizes the opportunities of tomorrow.”

“Saskatchewan is committed to protecting our economy despite uncertain global conditions," Saskatchewan’s Immigration and Career Training Minister Eric Schmalz said. "While Saskatchewan’s market and trade diversification has lessened the impact of global tariffs, we are happy to partner with the federal government to take a proactive approach to protecting Saskatchewan from potential risks to our economy, our labour market and our people.”

Quick facts

• Through the Canada-Saskatchewan Workforce Tariff Response, the governments of Canada and Saskatchewan are delivering targeted training and employment services funded through Employment Insurance contributions by workers and employers, helping workers return to the labour market more quickly.

• The Government of Canada also invests over $65 million annually through the Labour Market Development Agreements and the Workforce Development Agreements in Saskatchewan, which support training and employment services for approximately 32,000 people each year, including: 11,000 people who secure employment within about 6 months of receiving support; 12,000 youth (15 to 29 years old), 13,000 mid-career workers (35 to 54 years old) 11,000 workers from the trades, including 3,000 apprentices.

• At the national level, the agreement prepares workers to seize emerging opportunities, such as those that will be generated through national initiatives, including the Major Projects Office, Build Canada Homes, and the Defense Industrial Strategy.

• As of February 2026, Saskatchewan’s unemployment rate stands at 5.6 per cent, the lowest among provinces and significantly lower than the national average.

- 30 -

Associated links

• The Workforce Tariff Response: Preparing Canadian workers for the jobs of the future .

• Prime Minister Carney announces new measures to protect and strengthen Canada’s steel industry .

• Prime Minister Carney announces new measures to transform Canada’s softwood lumber industry .

• Prime Minister Carney launches new measures to protect, build, and transform Canadian strategic industries .

• Minister Hajdu announces actions to grow and protect workers .

Media inquiries

Media Relations Immigration and Career Training Government of Saskatchewan Email: [email protected] Phone: 306-798-2369
items.bodyZh:
加拿大和萨斯喀彻温省政府合作保护受关税影响的工人并加强劳动力队伍

发布于2026年3月27日

全球贸易格局正在迅速变化，为加拿大各地的工人、产业和社区造成了不确定性和挑战。在一个快速变化的世界中，加拿大必须专注于自己能够控制的因素：建设一个更强大、更有韧性的国家。这需要各级政府采取果断行动，保护就业机会，加强地方经济，并确保工人能够适应不断变化的经济现实。

因全球关税直接或间接受到影响的工人将获得支持，帮助他们适应、重新培训并获得成功。这是由加拿大荣誉部长Buckley Bélanger（代表就业和家庭部部长兼联邦北安大略经济发展机构负责人Patty Hajdu部长发言）与萨斯喀彻温省政府关系部部长、移民和职业培训部部长Eric Schmalz部长共同宣布的合作伙伴协议。

具体而言，"加拿大-萨斯喀彻温州劳动力关税应对计划"(Canada-Saskatchewan Workforce Tariff Response)将在三年内投入1560万加元，以支持钢铁、软木材以及其他直接和间接受关税影响产业的工人和雇主。预计这笔新资金将帮助萨斯喀彻温州约1800名工人建立新技能并抓住新兴机遇。

支持将通过现有的全省SaskJobs办公室和培训合作伙伴网络提供，确保可能从再培训或就业援助中受益的工人能够及时获得支持，因为他们转向新的机遇，包括：

• 失业工人为急需的工作岗位建立技能；

• 雇主参与就业保险(EI)工作分享协议的工人，使其能够在这些行业适应过程中提升技能或重新培训；以及

• 在直接受关税和全球市场变化影响的公司或供应链内加强技能和韧性的在职工人。

合作伙伴协议的实施将利用现有的以及新增或增强的项目和服务。与受影响的企业直接协调以保护就业机会，将为受关税影响的工人和参与就业保险工作分享协议的工人在不断变化的经济格局中提供更多的提升技能或重新培训的机会。

这一方法反映了加拿大和萨斯喀彻温州政府在支持加拿大人度过重大经济调整期间的共同承诺，同时建设一支强大、自信的劳动力队伍——一支能够应对全球不确定性、产业能够在全球市场上保持竞争力的队伍。

声明

就业和家庭部部长兼联邦北安大略经济发展机构负责人Patty Hajdu表示："当雇主和培训合作伙伴共同努力时，加拿大的劳动力最为强大。随着全球贸易的发展，没有工人会被落下。通过劳动力关税应对计划，这意味着为工人提供他们获得好工作所需的工具和培训，继续建设强大的社区，并确保加拿大在面对全球挑战时保持韧性。"

农村发展国务秘书Buckley Belanger表示："今天的合作伙伴关系标志着我们承诺支持受全球关税影响工人的重大进展。通过投资再培训和技能发展，我们正在赋能个人适应不断变化的经济，并确保他们能够在新机遇中蓬勃发展。与我们的省级合作伙伴一起，我们正在培养一支有韧性的劳动力队伍，不仅能够应对当今的挑战，还能抓住明天的机遇。"

萨斯喀彻温州移民和职业培训部部长Eric Schmalz表示："尽管全球形势不确定，萨斯喀彻温州仍致力于保护我们的经济。虽然萨斯喀彻温州市场和贸易多元化已经减少了全球关税的影响，但我们很高兴与联邦政府合作，采取积极主动的方式保护萨斯喀彻温州免受对我们的经济、劳动力市场和人民可能构成的风险。"

快速事实

• 通过加拿大-萨斯喀彻温州劳动力关税应对计划，加拿大和萨斯喀彻温州政府正在提供有针对性的培训和就业服务，资金来自工人和雇主的就业保险缴款，帮助工人更快地重返劳动力市场。

• 加拿大政府还通过劳动力市场发展协议(Labour Market Development Agreements)和劳动力发展协议(Workforce Development Agreements)在萨斯喀彻温州年投资超过6500万加元，支持每年约32000人的培训和就业服务，包括：11000人在获得支持后约6个月内获得就业机会；12000名青年（15至29岁），13000名中期职业工人（35至54岁），11000名来自工种的工人，包括3000名学徒。

• 在全国层面，该协议为工人准备抓住新兴机遇，例如那些将通过全国性举措(包括主要项目办公室、加拿大建筑项目和国防产业战略)产生的机遇。

• 截至2026年2月，萨斯喀彻温州失业率为5.6%，为各省最低，明显低于全国平均水平。

-完-

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited in the Greater Toronto Area ( GTA )
items.bodyEn:
On March 25, 2026, we issued 431 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker or International Student streams. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in the Greater Toronto Area ( GTA ).

The Greater Toronto Area ( GTA ) includes the following Census Divisions: Durham, Halton, Peel, Toronto, and York.

Candidates with a score of 61 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 00012 - Senior managers - financial, communications and other business services

• NOC 20010 - Engineering managers

• NOC 20011 - Architecture and science managers

• NOC 20012 - Computer and information systems managers

• NOC 21200 - Architects

• NOC 21222 - Information systems specialists

• NOC 21230 - Computer systems developers and programmers

• NOC 21231 - Software engineers and designers

• NOC 21232 - Software developers and programmers

• NOC 21233 - Web designers

• NOC 21234 - Web developers and programmers

• NOC 21311 - Computer engineers (except software engineers and designers)

• NOC 21331 - Geological engineers

• NOC 22222 - Information systems testing technicians

• NOC 30010 - Managers in health care

• NOC 31102 - General practitioners and family physicians

• NOC 31301 - Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses

• NOC 70010 - Construction managers

Candidates with a score of 90 and above received an invitation under the International Student stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 20010 - Engineering managers

• NOC 20012 - Computer and information systems managers

• NOC 21222 - Information systems specialists

• NOC 21231 - Software engineers and designers

• NOC 21232 - Software developers and programmers

• NOC 21234 - Web developers and programmers

• NOC 21311 - Computer engineers (except software engineers and designers)

• NOC 22222 - Information systems testing technicians

• NOC 31120 - Pharmacists

• NOC 31301 - Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses

• NOC 32129 - Other medical technologists and technicians

• NOC 70010 - Construction managers

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by March 23, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Your employer must review the employer guide and submit their application within 14 calendar days.

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX . You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.

items.bodyZh:
**标题：雇主工作邀请流：中安大略地区（不含大多伦多地区）邀请候选人**

2026年3月25日，我们向199名候选人发出申请邀请。这些候选人可能符合雇主工作邀请流（Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker、International Student或In-Demand Skills）的资格要求。候选人必须目前在加拿大居住，持有有效的工作或学习许可证，并在中安大略地区（不含大多伦多地区）拥有工作邀请。

中安大略地区（不含大多伦多地区）包括以下普查分区：Dufferin、Grey、Simcoe、Waterloo和Wellington。

**外国工人流（Foreign Worker Stream）：**

分数达到55分及以上的候选人获得外国工人流下的邀请。候选人必须拥有以下国家职业分类（National Occupational Classification，NOC）代码之一的工作邀请：

• NOC 10010 - 财务经理（Financial managers）

• NOC 10019 - 其他行政服务经理（Other administrative services managers）

• NOC 10022 - 广告、营销和公关经理（Advertising, marketing and public relations managers）

• NOC 12011 - 财务和保险办公室工作人员主管（Supervisors, finance and insurance office workers）

• NOC 12013 - 供应链、追踪和计划协调职业主管（Supervisors, supply chain, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations）

• NOC 12101 - 人力资源和招聘官员（Human resources and recruitment officers）

• NOC 12102 - 采购和购买代理人及官员（Procurement and purchasing agents and officers）

• NOC 20010 - 工程经理（Engineering managers）

• NOC 30010 - 医疗保健经理（Managers in health care）

• NOC 31301 - 注册护士和注册精神科护士（Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses）

• NOC 32101 - 持证实用护士（Licensed practical nurses）

• NOC 32201 - 按摩治疗师（Massage therapists）

• NOC 72022 - 印刷及相关职业主管（Supervisors, printing and related occupations）

**国际学生流（International Student Stream）：**

分数达到82分及以上的候选人获得国际学生流下的邀请。候选人必须拥有以下国家职业分类（NOC）代码之一的工作邀请：

• NOC 11201 - 商业管理咨询专业职业（Professional occupations in business management consulting）

• NOC 12013 - 供应链、追踪和计划协调职业主管（Supervisors, supply chain, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations）

• NOC 12101 - 人力资源和招聘官员（Human resources and recruitment officers）

• NOC 12102 - 采购和购买代理人及官员（Procurement and purchasing agents and officers）

• NOC 12200 - 会计技术人员和簿记员（Accounting technicians and bookkeepers）

• NOC 13100 - 行政官员（Administrative officers）

• NOC 13112 - 医疗行政助理（Medical administrative assistants）

• NOC 20012 - 计算机和信息系统经理（Computer and information systems managers）

• NOC 31301 - 注册护士和注册精神科护士（Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses）

• NOC 32101 - 持证实用护士（Licensed practical nurses）

• NOC 33100 - 牙科助理和牙科实验室助理（Dental assistants and dental laboratory assistants）

• NOC 33102 - 护理助理、医疗助手和患者服务助理（Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates）

• NOC 70010 - 建筑经理（Construction managers）

• NOC 72020 - 机械贸易承包商和主管（Contractors and supervisors, mechanic trades）

• NOC 73201 - 普通维护工人和建筑主管（General maintenance workers and building superintendents）

• NOC 73301 - 公共汽车司机、地铁运营员和其他公交运营员（Bus drivers, subway operators and other transit operators）

• NOC 92013 - 塑料和橡胶产品制造主管（Supervisors, plastic and rubber products manufacturing）

**需求技能流（In-Demand Skills Stream）：**

分数达到32分及以上的候选人获得需求技能流下的邀请。候选人必须拥有以下国家职业分类（NOC）代码之一的工作邀请：

• NOC 44101 - 家庭支持工人、管家和相关职业（Home support workers, housekeepers and related occupations）

符合条件的申请档案必须在2026年3月23日晚11:59前创建并通过认证。

**申请步骤：**

• 查阅雇主工作邀请流页面，确定您是否符合要求，并确保您拥有必需文件。

• 您的雇主必须查阅雇主指南，并在14个日历日内提交其申请。

• 登录安大略省移民提名计划电子申报门户（OINP e-Filing Portal），点击以JOXX为前缀的新创建的文件号。您必须在收到邀请之日起17个日历日内提交申请和付款。
items.summaryZh: 2026年3月25日，安大略省移民提名计划向199名候选人发出申请邀请，他们可能符合雇主工作邀请流（Foreign Worker、International Student或In-Demand Skills）的资格要求。受邀者需在中安大略地区（不含大多伦多地区）拥有工作邀请，并持有效工作或学习许可证在加拿大居住。根据不同流别，邀请分数从32分至82分不等，涵盖财务经理、护士、建筑经理等20多个国家职业分类代码。

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited in Southwestern Ontario

On March 25, 2026, we issued 251 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker or International Student streams. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in Southwestern Ontario.

Southwestern Ontario includes the following Census Divisions: Brant, Bruce, Chatham-Kent, Elgin, Essex, Haldimand-Norfolk, Hamilton, Hamilton-Wentworth, Huron, Lambton, Middlesex, Niagara, Oxford, and Perth.

Candidates with a score of 53 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

Candidates with a score of 81 and above received an invitation under the International Student stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by March 23, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Your employer must review the employer guide and submit their application within 14 calendar days.

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX . You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.

items.summaryZh: 加拿大安大略省移民项目2026年3月25日向251名候选人发放了雇主工作邀请邀请函,这些候选人符合外劳流（Foreign Worker stream）或国际留学生流（International Student stream）的资格,都在加拿大持有有效工作或学习许可,且在西南安大略地区获得了工作邀请。两个流的邀请分数线不同,涵盖的职业类别也有差异,外劳流包含管理、医疗、建筑等33个NOC代码,国际留学生流包含24个NOC代码。

Northern Ontario includes the following Census Divisions: Algoma, Cochrane, Greater Sudbury, Haliburton, Kenora, Manitoulin, Muskoka, Nipissing, Parry Sound, Rainy River, Sudbury, Thunder Bay, and Timiskaming.

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited in Eastern Ontario

Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in Eastern Ontario.

Eastern Ontario includes the following Census Divisions: Frontenac, Hastings, Kawartha Lakes, Lanark, Leeds and Grenville, Lennox and Addington , Northumberland, Ottawa, Ottawa-Carleton, Peterborough, Prescott and Russell, Prince Edward, Renfrew, and Stormont, Dundas and Glengarry.

Candidates with a score of 57 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 10010 - Financial managers

• NOC 10011 - Human resources managers

• NOC 10019 - Other administrative services managers

• NOC 10022 - Advertising, marketing and public relations managers

• NOC 11200 - Human resources professionals

• NOC 11201 - Professional occupations in business management consulting

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX . You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.

items.title: Saskatchewan Highlights Protections Against Immigration Fraud
items.bodyEn:
Released on March 23, 2026

March is Fraud Prevention Month in Canada, and the Government of Saskatchewan is protecting Saskatchewan people by preventing immigration fraud in the province. Since The Immigration Services Act (ISA) was proclaimed in 2024, Saskatchewan has strengthened immigration enforcement and compliance, resulting in four individuals charged, five administrative monetary penalties issued and one compensation order has been collected.

"We are committed to holding bad actors accountable when it comes to immigration fraud," Immigration and Career Training Minister Eric Schmalz said. "I want to be clear, it will not be tolerated here in Saskatchewan. March is Fraud Prevention Month, but we take the integrity of our immigration system seriously every day."

Protecting the integrity of our immigration system here in Saskatchewan not only protects vulnerable newcomers, but it also protects the reputation of our province as a destination of choice and it protects the reputation of law-abiding employers who use the immigration system properly.

Five monetary penalties were issued in 2025 for a total of $27,000 in fines. The penalties were issued for the offence of providing immigration and recruitment services without a license. The four charges were the first of a kind under provincial legislation in Canada. This includes charges laid under The Foreign Worker Recruitment and Immigration Services Act (FWRISA) and the ISA. The province has also issued four compensation orders and received payment for one totaling $96,000.

The Program Compliance Branch of the Ministry of Immigration and Career Training works to protect immigrants and foreign workers in Saskatchewan. Contact the Program Compliance Branch at [email protected] or call 306-798-1350 if you have questions about the ISA, believe your rights have been violated, or want to file a complaint about the immigration or recruitment process. You do not need permission from anyone to contact the Program Compliance Branch and all your information will be kept confidential.

While the ISA specifically protects against the unique situations facing immigrants and foreign workers, all employees, including Canadian citizens or permanent and temporary residents, are protected by employment standards and occupational health and safety provisions within The Saskatchewan Employment Act .

A full list of employers and immigration consultants who have been found in violation of the ISA is available here: https://www.saskatchewan.ca/residents/moving-to-saskatchewan/live-in-saskatchewan/by-immigrating/protections-for-immigrants-and-foreign-workers/violations-and-investigation-outcomes .

-30-
items.bodyZh:
萨斯喀彻温省强调防范移民欺诈的保护措施

发布日期：2026年3月23日

3月是加拿大的欺诈防范月，萨斯喀彻温省政府通过防止该省移民欺诈来保护萨省人民。自《移民服务法》(ISA)于2024年生效以来，萨省加强了移民执法和合规工作，已有4人被起诉、5份行政罚款被发出，且已收取1份赔偿令。

"我们承诺对移民欺诈的不法分子追究责任，"移民和职业培训部部长埃里克·施马尔茨(Eric Schmalz)表示。"我要明确表示，这在萨省是绝不容忍的。3月是欺诈防范月，但我们每天都认真对待移民系统的完整性。"

在萨斯喀彻温省保护移民系统的完整性不仅保护了易受伤害的新移民，还保护了我们省作为优选目的地的声誉，以及那些正确使用移民系统的守法雇主的声誉。

2025年共发出5份罚款单，罚款总额为27,000加元。罚款是因为无证提供移民和招聘服务的违法行为而发出的。这4项起诉是加拿大省级立法中首次出现的同类指控。这包括根据《外国劳工招聘和移民服务法》(FWRISA)和《移民服务法》(ISA)提起的指控。该省还发出了4份赔偿令，已收到1份赔偿令，金额为96,000加元。

移民和职业培训部的项目合规分支致力于保护在萨斯喀彻温省的移民和外国工人。如您对《移民服务法》有疑问、认为您的权利受到侵犯，或想就移民或招聘过程提出投诉，请通过电子邮件[email protected]或致电306-798-1350联系项目合规分支。您无需任何人的许可即可联系项目合规分支，我们将对您的所有信息保密。

虽然《移民服务法》专门保护移民和外国工人面临的独特情况，但所有员工，包括加拿大公民、永久居民或临时居民，都受《萨斯喀彻温省就业法》(The Saskatchewan Employment Act)内就业标准和职业健康与安全条款的保护。

违反《移民服务法》的雇主和移民顾问的完整名单可在以下网址查看：https://www.saskatchewan.ca/residents/moving-to-saskatchewan/live-in-saskatchewan/by-immigrating/protections-for-immigrants-and-foreign-workers/violations-and-investigation-outcomes 。

items.title: Victim Compensated $96,000 Under Immigration Services Act
items.bodyEn:
Released on March 20, 2026

The Ministry of Immigration and Career Training issued a compensation order of $96,000 against Yan Wang for offences under The Immigration Services Act (ISA).

Wang, an unlicensed immigration consultant, collected payment for immigration services but failed to submit any documents on the client's behalf to the Saskatchewan Immigrant Nominee Program and provided the victim with falsified documentation.

Following a formal investigation, the Ministry exercised its statutory authority to issue a compensation order against Yan Wang, resulting in the full recovery of funds owed to the victim, who suffered financial losses as a result of the unauthorized and fraudulent immigration services provided by Wang. This is the first compensation order that has been paid under the ISA.

Under ISA, the Ministry of Immigration and Career Training is authorized to investigate complaints, issue compensation orders, or administrative penalties, and enforce those orders against immigration consultants who engage in fraudulent or negligent conduct. Consultants who provide services without a license, produce false documents, or otherwise exploit clients face serious consequences under the ISA.

-30-
items.bodyZh:
标题：受害人根据《移民服务法》获赔96,000加元

发布日期：2026年3月20日

移民和职业培训部（Ministry of Immigration and Career Training）根据《移民服务法》（The Immigration Services Act, ISA）的相关违法行为，对严晓旺（Yan Wang）发出了96,000加元的赔偿令。

严晓旺是一名无执照移民顾问，他收取了移民服务费用，但未能代表客户向萨斯喀彻温省移民提名计划（Saskatchewan Immigrant Nominee Program）提交任何文件，并向受害人提供了伪造文件。

经过正式调查，移民和职业培训部行使其法定权力对严晓旺发出赔偿令，使受害人得以全额追回因严晓旺提供的非法和欺诈性移民服务而遭受的经济损失。这是《移民服务法》下首份已执行的赔偿令。

根据《移民服务法》，移民和职业培训部被授权调查投诉、发出赔偿令或行政罚款，并对从事欺诈或过失行为的移民顾问强制执行这些令。未持证提供服务、制造虚假文件或以其他方式欺骗客户的顾问将在《移民服务法》下面临严重后果。

-30-
items.summaryZh: 无执照移民顾问诈骗客户9.6万加元，被责令赔偿。加拿大移民部门依据《移民服务法》对违法顾问发出首份赔偿令，受害客户成功追回全部损失。

items.title: Protecting our Economy by Investing in the Workforce of Tomorrow
items.bodyEn:
Released on March 18, 2026

The Ministry of Immigration and Career Training is investing nearly $125 million in programs and services to prepare Saskatchewan people for, and ensure they are the first to benefit from, the high-quality jobs being created by our economy.

"Our government will always work to ensure that the individuals living and working in our province have access to the employment opportunities being created by our strong and growing economy," Immigration and Career Training Minister Eric Schmalz said. "We will continue to protect Saskatchewan workers and employers by investing in programs that help Saskatchewan citizens gain the skills and education required to secure employment."

"Our government will always work to ensure that the individuals living and working in our province have access to the employment opportunities being created by our strong and growing economy," Immigration and Career Training Minister Eric Schmalz said. "We will continue to protect Saskatchewan workers and employers by investing in programs that help Saskatchewan citizens gain the skills and education required to secure employment."

The 2026-27 Provincial Budget includes funding for workforce development programs that support unemployed and underemployed people, including:

• $21.8 million for adult essential skills training, which provides people with the foundational skills and abilities they need to get a job, such as literacy and job interviewing skills;

• $22.2 million for training programs delivered through post-secondary institutions that provide people with the skills and credentials they need to work in in-demand occupations in sectors such as health care, construction, mining and manufacturing;

• $25.9 million for pre-employment programs and services which are offered through third-party partnerships and include work placement opportunities and career counselling; and

• $10.4 million to support persons with disabilities to prepare for transition into the workforce.

An increase of $2 million will add 300 apprenticeship training seats for skilled trades people, bringing the total investment in apprenticeship training to $27.2 million annually and increasing the total number of training seats to 5,300. A corresponding increase of $194,000 is being made in the Apprenticeship Training Allowance (ATA) to ensure apprentices are funded to participate in training away from their home community. This brings the total ATA investment to $3.43 million annually.

Also included is an increase of $534,000 to the Regina and Saskatoon Trades and Skills Centres, bringing the ministry's total investment to $3.5 million. This increase will allow each of Regina and Saskatoon's Centres to offer three more training courses per year.

2026-27 Budget includes an increase of $9.2 million in funding through the Labour Market Transfer Agreement (LMTA) with the federal government. This targeted support will protect Saskatchewan workers and businesses from tariff impacts. It will be used to support intensive training for unemployed workers or training for employees on work-sharing agreements to protect them from job loss. The ministry will work with affected employers to design and deliver training programs, including employer-sponsored training to assist in employee reskilling and upskilling.

The budget supports the province's Patients First Health Care Plan with an investment of $3.7 million for training and settlement supports for in-demand health occupations, which includes:

• $2.8 million to support training seats for continuing care assistants, licensed practical nurses, registered psychiatric nurses and diagnostic medical sonographers; and

• $910,000 for credential recognition programming to support workforce development and retention initiatives.

The Ministry of Immigration and Career Training's 2026-27 Budget is investing in programs and services in support of the three-point plan outlined in Building the Workforce for a Growing Economy: the Saskatchewan Labour Market Strategy . By preparing Saskatchewan people for jobs, recognizing skills in Saskatchewan, and through the use of targeted international recruitment when jobs cannot be filled by local talent, Saskatchewan employers will have the labour force required to grow our economy.

-30-
items.bodyZh:
**保护我们的经济，投资明天的劳动力队伍**

2026年3月18日发布

移民和职业培训部(Ministry of Immigration and Career Training)正在投资近1.25亿加元用于各种项目和服务，以为萨斯喀彻温省居民做好准备，确保他们首先受益于我们经济所创造的高质量工作。

"我们政府将始终致力于确保在我们省生活和工作的居民能够获得由我们强劲增长的经济所创造的就业机会，"移民和职业培训部部长埃里克·施马尔茨(Eric Schmalz)表示。"我们将继续通过投资能帮助萨斯喀彻温省公民获得所需的技能和教育以确保就业的项目，来保护萨斯喀彻温省的工人和雇主。"

"我们政府将始终致力于确保在我们省生活和工作的居民能够获得由我们强劲增长的经济所创造的就业机会，"移民和职业培训部部长埃里克·施马尔茨(Eric Schmalz)表示。"我们将继续通过投资能帮助萨斯喀彻温省公民获得所需的技能和教育以确保就业的项目，来保护萨斯喀彻温省的工人和雇主。"

2026-27年度省级预算包括支持失业和就业不足人员的劳动力发展项目的资金，包括：

• 2,180万加元用于成人基础技能培训，为人们提供获得工作所需的基础技能和能力，如识字和求职面试技能；

• 2,220万加元用于通过高等教育机构提供的培训项目，为人们提供在医疗保健、建筑、采矿和制造等部门紧缺职业中工作所需的技能和证书；

• 2,590万加元用于就业前项目和服务，这些项目和服务通过第三方合作伙伴提供，包括工作安置机会和职业咨询；以及

• 1,040万加元用于支持残障人士为进入劳动力市场做准备。

增加200万加元将新增300个熟练技工学徒培训席位，使学徒培训的年度总投资达到2,720万加元，并将培训席位总数增加至5,300个。学徒培训补助金(Apprenticeship Training Allowance, ATA)相应增加194,000加元，以确保学徒获得资金支持在离家社区参加培训。这使ATA的年度总投资达到343万加元。

此外，还增加534,000加元投入里贾纳和萨斯卡通技工和技能中心，使该部的总投资达到350万加元。这一增加将使里贾纳和萨斯卡通的两个中心每年各额外提供三门培训课程。

2026-27年度预算包括通过与联邦政府的劳动力市场转移协议(Labour Market Transfer Agreement, LMTA)增加920万加元的资金。这一有针对性的支持将保护萨斯喀彻温省工人和企业免受关税影响。这笔资金将用于为失业工人提供强化培训或为参加工作分享协议的员工提供培训，以保护他们免于失业。该部将与受影响的雇主合作设计和提供培训项目，包括雇主赞助的培训以协助员工重新培训和提升技能。

预算支持该省的《患者优先医疗保健计划》(Patients First Health Care Plan)，为紧缺医疗职业的培训和安置支持投资370万加元，包括：

• 280万加元用于支持持续护理助理、执业护士、注册精神科护士和诊断医学超声医学技师的培训席位；以及

• 91万加元用于证书认可项目，以支持劳动力发展和保留计划。

移民和职业培训部的2026-27年度预算投资于《为增长经济打造劳动力队伍：萨斯喀彻温省劳动力市场战略》(Building the Workforce for a Growing Economy: the Saskatchewan Labour Market Strategy)中概述的三点计划所支持的项目和服务。通过为萨斯喀彻温省居民做好工作准备、在萨斯喀彻温省认可技能，以及在本地人才无法填补职位时有针对性地进行国际招聘，萨斯喀彻温省雇主将拥有发展我们经济所需的劳动力。

items.summaryZh: 萨斯喀彻温省政府宣布投入近1.25亿加元用于劳动力培训和就业项目，帮助本地居民获得经济增长创造的高质量就业机会。这笔投资覆盖成人基础技能培训、职业教育、就业前服务、学徒培训等多个领域，同时增加对残障人士和医疗卫生职业培训的支持。

items.title: Masters and PhD Graduate streams: candidates invited
items.bodyEn:
On March 18, 2026, we issued 582 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify under the Masters Graduate stream. We issued 525 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify under the PhD Graduate stream. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit.

Candidates with a score of 30 and above received an invitation under the Masters Graduate stream. Candidates must have Canadian work experience in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 00010 - Legislators

Candidates with a score of 49 and above received an invitation under the PhD Graduate stream. Candidates must have Canadian work experience in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by March 16, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Masters Graduate or PhD Graduate stream pages to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix NMAS or NPHD . You must submit your application and payment within 14 calendar days.

items.summaryZh: 加拿大安省移民提名项目（OINP）在2026年3月18日分别向符合硕士研究生流（Masters Graduate stream）和博士研究生流（PhD Graduate stream）条件的候选人发出申请邀请，共计1107份邀请。申请人必须当前居住在加拿大且持有有效的工作或学习许可，并在指定的职业代码中拥有加拿大工作经验。

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited under the Regional Economic Development through Immigration ( REDI ) pilot
items.bodyEn:
On March 18, 2026, we issued 39 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify for the REDI pilot under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker , International Student or In-Demand Skills streams. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in Lanark or Leeds and Grenville.

Candidates with a score of 47 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 11100 - Financial auditors and accountants

• NOC 22302 - Industrial engineering and manufacturing technologists and technicians

• NOC 32104 - Animal health technologists and veterinary technicians

• NOC 33102 - Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates

• NOC 72302 - Gas fitters

• NOC 72410 - Automotive service technicians, truck and bus mechanics and mechanical repairers

Candidates with a score of 61 and above received an invitation under the International Student stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 33102 - Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates

• NOC 42201 - Social and community service workers

• NOC 42202 - Early childhood educators and assistants

• NOC 70010 - Construction managers

• NOC 72100 - Machinists and machining and tooling inspectors

• NOC 73202 - Pest controllers and fumigators

Candidates with a score of 30 and above received an invitation under the In-Demand Skills stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 44101 - Home support workers, housekeepers and related occupations

• NOC 94100 - Machine operators, mineral and metal processing

• NOC 94111 - Plastics processing machine operators

• NOC 94140 - Process control and machine operators, food and beverage processing

• NOC 94201 - Electronics assemblers, fabricators, inspectors and testers

• NOC 95106 - Labourers in food and beverage processing

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by March 16, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Your employer must review the employer guide and submit their application within 14 calendar days.

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX . You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.
items.bodyZh:
2026年3月18日，我们向39名可能符合REDI试点资格的申请人发放了申请邀请，他们可通过以下雇主工作机会流申请：外籍员工流（Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker）、国际学生流（International Student）或紧缺技能流（In-Demand Skills）。申请人必须目前在加拿大居住且持有有效工作或学习许可，并在Lanark或Leeds and Grenville地区持有工作机会。

在外籍员工流下，分数达到47分及以上的申请人获得邀请。申请人必须持有以下国家职业分类（NOC，National Occupational Classification）代码之一的工作机会：

• NOC 11100 - 财务审计员和会计师

• NOC 22302 - 工业工程和制造技术人员和技术员

• NOC 32104 - 动物卫生技术人员和兽医技术员

• NOC 33102 - 护理员、医院勤务员和患者服务助理

• NOC 72302 - 燃气管道装配工

• NOC 72410 - 汽车维修技术员、卡车和巴士维修技师及机械维修人员

在国际学生流下，分数达到61分及以上的申请人获得邀请。申请人必须持有以下国家职业分类（NOC）代码之一的工作机会：

• NOC 33102 - 护理员、医院勤务员和患者服务助理

• NOC 42201 - 社会和社区服务工作者

• NOC 42202 - 幼儿教育工作者和助理

• NOC 70010 - 建筑经理

• NOC 72100 - 机械师和机械加工及工具检验员

• NOC 73202 - 害虫防治员和熏蒸工

在紧缺技能流下，分数达到30分及以上的申请人获得邀请。申请人必须持有以下国家职业分类（NOC）代码之一的工作机会：

• NOC 44101 - 家庭支持工作者、管家及相关职业

• NOC 94100 - 机器操作员、矿物和金属加工

• NOC 94111 - 塑料加工机器操作员

• NOC 94140 - 工艺控制和机器操作员、食品和饮料加工

• NOC 94201 - 电子产品装配工、制造工、检验员和测试员

• NOC 95106 - 食品和饮料加工劳动者

符合条件的档案必须在2026年3月16日晚上11:59前创建并获得认证。

申请流程：

• 查看雇主工作机会流页面以确定您是否满足要求，并确保您拥有必要文件。

• 您的雇主必须查看雇主指南，并在14个日历日内提交申请。

• 登录OINP电子备案门户（OINP e-Filing Portal），点击前缀为JOXX的新创建档案号。您必须在收到邀请之日起17个日历日内提交申请和付款。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大安省移民局在2026年3月18日向39名符合REDI试点条件的申请人发放邀请，涉及雇主工作机会流（外籍员工、国际学生或紧缺技能）。申请人必须持有效工作或学习许可在加拿大居住，并在Lanark或Leeds and Grenville地区持有工作机会。

items.title: Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream: physicians invited
items.bodyEn:
On March 18, 2026, we issued 97 invitations to apply to physicians who may qualify for the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream .

Candidates with a score of 36 and above received an invitation. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 31100 - Specialists in clinical and laboratory medicine

• NOC 31101 - Specialists in surgery

• NOC 31102 - General practitioners and family physicians

Please refer to the January 5 program update for more details.

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by March 16, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX . You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days.
items.bodyZh:
2026年3月18日，我们向97名可能符合雇主工作邀请：外国劳工流(Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream)条件的医生发出了申请邀请。

分数达到36分及以上的候选人收到了邀请。候选人必须持有以下国家职业分类(National Occupational Classification, NOC)代码之一的工作邀请：

• NOC 31100 - 临床和实验室医学专科医生(Specialists in clinical and laboratory medicine)

• NOC 31101 - 外科专科医生(Specialists in surgery)

• NOC 31102 - 全科医生和家庭医生(General practitioners and family physicians)

请参阅1月5日的项目更新以获取更多详情。

符合条件的候选人档案必须在2026年3月16日晚上11:59之前创建并确认。

申请步骤：

• 查看雇主工作邀请流(Employer Job Offer stream)页面，确定您是否符合要求并确保您拥有所有强制性文件。

• 登录OINP电子申报门户(OINP e-Filing Portal)，点击前缀为JOXX的新建文件号。您必须在17个日历天内提交申请和支付费用。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大安省于2026年3月18日向97名医生发出邀请，邀请他们申请雇主工作邀请：外国劳工流(Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream)项目，分数达36分及以上的候选人获得邀请资格，申请人必须持有指定医疗职业代码的工作邀请。

items.title: Updates to Worker Expression of Interest
items.bodyEn:
On February 25, 2026, new fields were introduced to the Worker Expression of Interest (WEOI) to collect information on wages and hours of work for candidates with an Alberta job offer. WEOIs submitted on or after this date must include this information. WEOIs submitted before this date will not contain this information.

These factors may be taken into consideration for future draws, and WEOIs missing this information will not be considered in those draws.
items.bodyZh:
**工人兴趣表达更新**

2026年2月25日，工人兴趣表达（Worker Expression of Interest, WEOI）中引入了新字段，用于收集有阿尔伯塔省工作offer的候选人的工资和工作时数信息。在此日期或之后提交的WEOI必须包含这些信息。在此日期之前提交的WEOI将不包含这些信息。

这些因素可能被纳入未来抽签的考量范围，缺少这些信息的WEOI将不符合这些抽签的资格。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大在2026年2月25日起要求申请者在「工人兴趣表达」中提供工资和工作时数信息（针对有阿尔伯塔省工作offer的候选人），缺少这些信息的申请将不符合未来的抽签资格。

items.bodyZh:
三月是欺诈防范月

items.title: NSNP Update: Four Consolidated Streams
items.bodyEn:
The Nova Scotia Nominee Program (NSNP) has modernized its immigration streams to create clearer pathways for newcomers and employers and to streamline processing.

What has Changed

Ten NSNP immigration streams, each with different eligibility requirements based on occupation, work experience, education and language ability, have been consolidated into a new structure with four streams. As of February 18, 2026, the NSNP streams are:

• Nova Scotia Graduate

• Skilled Worker

• Entrepreneur

• Nova Scotia: Express Entry

Eligibility Remains the Same

These streams include sub-criteria that mirror the eligibility requirements of the old streams.

Skilled Worker:

• The Construction Worker sub-criteria are the same eligibility requirements as the former Critical Construction Worker Pilot.

• The Physician sub-criteria are the same eligibility requirements as the Physician Stream.

Entrepreneur:

• The International Graduate sub-criteria are the same eligibility requirements as the International Graduate Entrepreneur Stream.

Nova Scotia Graduate:

• The eligibility requirements for International Graduates in Demand are included in the new Nova Scotia Graduate Stream.

Nova Scotia: Express Entry:

• The former Nova Scotia Experience: Express Entry, Labour Market Priorities and Labour Market Priorities for Physicians Streams have been consolidated into the new Nova Scotia: Express Entry Stream. This stream includes all the same eligibility requirements.

New Submissions

• These changes apply only to new Expression of Interest (EOI) submissions submitted to the NSNP after February 18, 2026.

• The changes do not affect the Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP).

Existing EOI Submissions

• EOI submissions already in the NSNP pool remain active and are not affected.

• The consolidation of NSNP streams does not affect your eligibility.

• No action is required from you.

We appreciate your patience as we continue to improve the efficiency and clarity of our immigration programs.
items.bodyZh:
新斯科舍省提名计划(Nova Scotia Nominee Program, NSNP)已现代化其移民流，为新移民和雇主创造了更清晰的途径，并简化了审理流程。

**发生了什么变化**

十条NSNP移民流，各自基于职业、工作经验、教育和语言能力有不同的资格要求，已合并为具有四条流的新结构。自2026年2月18日起，NSNP流为：

• 新斯科舍省毕业生(Nova Scotia Graduate)

• 技术工人(Skilled Worker)

• 创业者(Entrepreneur)

• 新斯科舍省：快速通道(Nova Scotia: Express Entry)

**资格要求保持不变**

这些流包括反映旧流资格要求的次级标准。

技术工人(Skilled Worker)：

• 建筑工人(Construction Worker)次级标准与前关键建筑工人试点项目(Critical Construction Worker Pilot)的资格要求相同。

• 医生(Physician)次级标准与医生流(Physician Stream)的资格要求相同。

创业者(Entrepreneur)：

• 国际毕业生(International Graduate)次级标准与国际毕业生创业家流(International Graduate Entrepreneur Stream)的资格要求相同。

新斯科舍省毕业生(Nova Scotia Graduate)：

• 国际毕业生需求项目(International Graduates in Demand)的资格要求已纳入新的新斯科舍省毕业生流。

新斯科舍省：快速通道(Nova Scotia: Express Entry)：

• 前新斯科舍省经验：快速通道(Nova Scotia Experience: Express Entry)、劳动力市场优先项目(Labour Market Priorities)和医生劳动力市场优先项目(Labour Market Priorities for Physicians)流已合并为新的新斯科舍省：快速通道流。此流包括所有相同的资格要求。

**新的递交**

• 这些变化仅适用于2026年2月18日之后递交给NSNP的新意向表达(Expression of Interest, EOI)。

• 这些变化不影响大西洋移民计划(Atlantic Immigration Program, AIP)。

**现有EOI递交**

• 已在NSNP池中的EOI递交保持活跃，不受影响。

• NSNP流的合并不影响你的资格。

• 无需你采取任何行动。

我们感谢你的耐心，我们将继续改进移民计划的效率和清晰度。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大新斯科舍省提名计划(NSNP)将原有的10条移民流合并为4条，自2026年2月18日起生效。此举旨在为申请人和雇主提供更清晰的移民途径并加快审理，但现有申请不受影响，申请人无需采取任何行动。

items.title: Ontario’s 2026 nomination allocation
items.bodyEn:
The Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program ( OINP ) has received its 2026 nomination allocation from the federal government. The province’s 2026 allocation is 14,119 nominations.

Please monitor this page for news, announcements, and program developments.
items.bodyZh:
安大略移民提名计划(Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program, OINP)已收到联邦政府分配的2026年提名配额。该省2026年的配额为14,119个提名。

请关注此页面以获取新闻、公告和项目动态。

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited in healthcare and early childhood education occupations
items.bodyEn:
On February 2, 2026, we issued 1,649 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify for the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker or International Student streams who work in healthcare or early childhood education occupations. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit.

Candidates with a score of 36 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream, and 56 and above under the International Student stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 31300 - Nursing co-ordinators and supervisors

• NOC 31301 - Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses

• NOC 31302 - Nurse practitioners

• NOC 31303 - Physician assistants, midwives and allied health professionals

• NOC 32101 - Licensed practical nurses

• NOC 33102 - Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates

• NOC 42202 – Early childhood educators and assistants

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by January 28, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Your employer must review the employer guide and submit their application within 14 calendar days.

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX. You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.

items.title: Employer Job Offer streams: candidates invited under the Regional Economic Development through Immigration ( REDI ) pilot
items.bodyEn:
On February 2, 2026, we issued 47 invitations to apply to candidates who may qualify for the REDI pilot under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker , International Student or In-Demand Skills streams. Candidates must currently reside in Canada with a valid work or study permit and have a job offer in Lanark or Leeds and Grenville.

Candidates with a score of 44 and above received an invitation under the Foreign Worker stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 12200 - Accounting technicians and bookkeepers

• NOC 21222 - Information systems specialists

• NOC 21231 - Software engineers and designers

• NOC 31301 - Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses

• NOC 33102 - Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates

• NOC 42202 - Early childhood educators and assistants

Candidates with a score of 69 and above received an invitation under the International Student stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 12111 - Health information management occupations

• NOC 12200 - Accounting technicians and bookkeepers

• NOC 13111 - Legal administrative assistants

• NOC 22232 - Occupational health and safety specialists

• NOC 22310 - Electrical and electronics engineering technologists and technicians

• NOC 32101 - Licensed practical nurses

• NOC 33102 - Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates

• NOC 33103 - Pharmacy technical assistants and pharmacy assistants

• NOC 42201 - Social and community service workers

• NOC 42202 - Early childhood educators and assistants

• NOC 52120 - Graphic designers and illustrators

Candidates with a score of 34 and above received an invitation under the In-Demand Skills stream. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 44101 - Home support workers, housekeepers and related occupations

• NOC 75101 - Material handlers

• NOC 94100 - Machine operators, mineral and metal processing

• NOC 94111 - Plastics processing machine operators

• NOC 94140 - Process control and machine operators, food and beverage processing

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by January 28, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Your employer must review the employer guide and submit their application within 14 calendar days.

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX. You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days from the date we issued the invitation.
items.bodyZh:

得分44分及以上的申请人在外籍劳工流中获得邀请。申请人必须拥有以下国家职业分类(National Occupational Classification, NOC)代码之一的工作邀请:

得分69分及以上的申请人在国际学生流中获得邀请。申请人必须拥有以下国家职业分类(NOC)代码之一的工作邀请:

• NOC 12111 - 卫生信息管理职业

• NOC 12200 - 会计技术员和簿记员

得分34分及以上的申请人在紧缺技能流中获得邀请。申请人必须拥有以下国家职业分类(NOC)代码之一的工作邀请:

符合条件的档案必须在2026年1月28日晚上11:59之前创建并经证实。

申请方式:

• 您的雇主必须查看雇主指南,并在14个日历天内提交申请。

• 登录OINP电子申报门户,点击新创建的以JOXX为前缀的档案号。您必须在收到邀请之日起17个日历天内提交申请和付款。

items.title: Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream: physicians invited
items.bodyEn:
On February 2, 2026, we issued 129 invitations to apply to physicians who may qualify for the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream.

Candidates with a score of 33 and above received an invitation. Candidates must have a job offer in one of the following National Occupational Classification ( NOC ) codes:

• NOC 31100 - Specialists in clinical and laboratory medicine

• NOC 31101 - Specialists in surgery

• NOC 31102 - General practitioners and family physicians

Please refer to the January 5 program update for more details.

Eligible profiles must have been created and attested to by January 28, 2026, 11:59 p.m.

To apply:

• Review the Employer Job Offer stream page to determine if you meet the requirements and ensure you have the mandatory documents .

• Log in to the OINP e-Filing Portal and click the newly created file number with the prefix JOXX. You must submit your application and payment within 17 calendar days.

items.title: BC PNP updates Skills Immigration application fee
items.bodyEn:
BC PNP updates Skills Immigration application fee Effective January 22, 2026, the BC Provincial Nominee Program (BC PNP) is increasing the Skills Immigration application fee from $1,475 to $1,750. The BC PNP provides immigration pathways for skilled international workers and eligible foreign entrepreneurs seeking permanent residency for themselves and their dependents. It is a strategic immigration tool for attracting talent in high‑demand occupations and supporting regional economic growth in B.C. For detailed fee information, please visit the BC PNP For Workers page.

To see news posts from previous years, please visit the Archives page.
items.bodyZh:
BC PNP 技能移民申请费更新

自2026年1月22日起，BC Provincial Nominee Program（BC PNP，卑诗省提名项目）将技能移民类别的申请费从1,475加元提高至1,750加元。BC PNP 为寻求为自己及其受养人获得永久居民身份的 skilled international workers（技术工人）和 eligible foreign entrepreneurs（符合条件的外国企业家）提供移民途径。该项目是吸引高需求职业人才和支持卑诗省地区经济增长的战略性移民工具。如需详细的收费信息，请访问 BC PNP For Workers（BC PNP 工作者页面）。

如需查看往年新闻发布，请访问"档案"页面。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大卑诗省提名项目（BC PNP）的技能移民类别申请费将于2026年1月22日上调，从1,475加元增至1,750加元。该项目为寻求永久居民身份的skilled international workers（技术工人）和eligible foreign entrepreneurs（符合条件的外国企业家）提供移民途径。

items.title: Changes to eligibility for self-employed physicians under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream
items.bodyEn:
The Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program ( OINP ) has made changes to the classes of certificates of registration from the College of Physicians and Surgeons of Ontario ( CPSO ) that physicians can hold if applying as self-employed to the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream.

These changes aim to broaden eligibility for foreign national internationally educated physicians and are in alignment with changes to regulations made under the Medicine Act,1991 , which came into effect on January 1, 2026.

Physicians under National Occupational Classification codes 31100, 31101 and 31102 who are members in good standing with the College of Physicians and Surgeons of Ontario, and hold a provisional certificate of registration, are now eligible to apply as self-employed to the OINP for a certificate of nomination provided they have an Ontario Health Insurance Plan OHIP billing number.

The amendments also remove the postgraduate license from the list of eligible licenses since postgraduate license-holders are unable to meet the requirement of having an OHIP billing number. Postgraduate education license-holders may be eligible under the OINP ’s Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream if they have a job offer with an Ontario employer.

The amendments came into effect on January 1, 2026.

These changes are reflected on the program requirements webpage. For more information, please visit:

• OINP Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream

The updated regulations can be found on Ontario’s e-Laws Website:

• Ontario Regulation 422/17 (General)
items.bodyZh:
安大略省移民提名项目（Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program，OINP）对医生以自雇身份申请雇主工作邀请-外国劳工流（Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream）的资格条件进行了调整。

这些变化旨在扩大外国籍国际培养医学毕业生的申请资格，与2026年1月1日生效的《医学法案,1991》（Medicine Act,1991）监管条例变化相一致。

根据国家职业分类代码（National Occupational Classification codes）31100、31101和31102分类的医生，只要符合以下条件，现在即可以自雇身份向OINP申请提名证书：与安大略医学和外科医学院（College of Physicians and Surgeons of Ontario，CPSO）保持良好的会员资格、持有临时注册证书（provisional certificate of registration），以及持有安大略健康保险计划（Ontario Health Insurance Plan，OHIP）计费号。

该修订也将研究生执业执照从合格执照名单中删除，因为研究生执业执照持有人无法满足拥有OHIP计费号的要求。持有研究生教育执照的人员若拥有来自安大略雇主的工作邀请，则可能符合OINP雇主工作邀请-外国劳工流的资格条件。

修订条款自2026年1月1日起生效。

这些变化已在项目要求网页中更新。如需更多信息，请访问：

• OINP雇主工作邀请-外国劳工流（OINP Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream）

更新后的监管条例可在安大略省电子法律网站上查阅：

• 安大略法规422/17（通用）（Ontario Regulation 422/17 (General)）
items.summaryZh: 加拿大安大略省移民提名项目（OINP）调整了自雇医生在雇主工作邀请-外国劳工流（Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream）下的资格要求，新规于2026年1月1日生效。主要变化包括：扩大了持有安大略医学和外科医学院（CPSO）临时注册证书的国际培养医学毕业生的申请资格，但取消了持有研究生执业执照者的资格（因其无法获得安大略健康保险计划OHIP计费号）。

items.title: Processing Policy: Expression of Interest
items.bodyEn:
November 28, 2025

Nova Scotia is formalizing an expression of interest (EOI) process for all streams of the Nova Scotia Nominee Program (NSNP) and for Designations and Endorsements under the Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP). This important step will bring clarity and predictability to both applicants and employers, while ensuring the Province can respond to changing labour market needs and support sustainable, long-term community growth.

How does an EOI Process help Nova Scotia meet its labour needs?

The number of individuals interested in immigrating to Nova Scotia is significantly higher than the number of applications the province can approve under its annual federal allocation from Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC).

The EOI model is widely used across immigration systems in Canada. It enables Nova Scotia to identify and invite submissions that best align with the province’s evolving economic priorities, at the discretion of the department of Labour, Skills and Immigration (LSI). These priorities could include sectors such as healthcare, construction and trades, STEM, natural resources and manufacturing, along with broader community and workforce needs. Please continue to visit our website for published draw information.

The EOI model also sets clear expectations for the candidate and employer while providing a transparent and predictable experience.

How our EOI process works: NSNP and AIP

• Submit your expression of interest: Candidates (NSNP) and employers (AIP) will continue to submit full applications. These submissions are treated as EOIs and entered into an EOI pool.

• Draws for processing: Nova Scotia will conduct periodic draws to select EOIs for processing. Factors that may guide selection include provincial priorities, remaining allocation, EOI pool volume, and program integrity considerations.

• If selected: You will receive direct communication from our department confirming your submission has been chosen for processing. Being selected means your submission aligns with current priorities. It does not guarantee approval.

• If not selected: Your submission remains in the EOI pool. If you do not receive communication, your status has not changed.

Existing Submissions

All submissions to Nova Scotia’s immigration programs — regardless of when they were submitted — are considered EOIs. Meeting eligibility criteria or having a case ID does not guarantee that your submission will be processed.

Processing Notifications

We will contact you if your submission is selected for processing. If you have not heard from us, it remains in the EOI pool with no new updates. You do not need to inquire, as we will notify you if your submission status changes.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: How does this announcement impact my existing submission?

A: Your submission remains in the EOI pool, and no action is required. If it is selected for processing, you will receive direct communications from our department. Until then, the status of your submission has not changed, and we have no further updates to provide.

Q: When will my EOI be selected for processing?

A: We are unable to provide a timeline for the selection of EOIs for processing. We are unable to guarantee that your EOI will be selected for processing. You may wish to monitor our website for periodic draw information.

Q: Does this announcement impact AIP Designations?

A: Yes, AIP designation submissions are considered an EOI and will follow the same process outlined above.

Q: Am I still submitting a full application?

A: Yes. The requirement to submit a complete application remains unchanged. Please ensure your submission is complete to avoid a delay in processing.

Q: How long will my submission stay in the EOI pool?

A: Your submission will stay in the EOI pool until it is drawn, or until further notice. Timelines vary depending on the number of factors, including available spaces, labour market priorities and EOI volumes. Nova Scotia will contact you only if your submission is selected.

Q: If my submission is selected does that mean it will likely be approved?

A: There are no guarantees that your submission will be approved if it is selected for processing.

Q: How do I get updates on my file?

A: You will receive direct communication from our department confirming your submission has been chosen for processing. In the meantime, you can visit our website at liveinnovascotia.ca for the most up to date program information.

Q: What submissions will you be prioritizing?

A: Nova Scotia will prioritize submissions that align with current economic and labour market needs. Priority areas may include sectors such as healthcare, construction and trades, STEM, natural resources, manufacturing, and other workforce needs across the province. These priorities will evolve over time based on labour market data and government direction. Updated selection information will be published on our website.
items.bodyZh:
2025年11月28日

新斯科舍省正式推行意向表达(EOI, Expression of Interest)流程,适用于新斯科舍省提名计划(NSNP, Nova Scotia Nominee Program)的所有项目流以及大西洋移民计划(AIP, Atlantic Immigration Program)的指定和背书。这一重要举措将为申请人和雇主带来清晰度和可预测性,同时确保该省能够应对不断变化的劳动力市场需求,并支持可持续的长期社区增长。

意向表达流程如何帮助新斯科舍省满足其劳动力需求?

有意移居新斯科舍省的申请人数量远高于该省从加拿大移民、难民和公民部(IRCC, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada)的年度联邦配额中能够批准的申请数量。

意向表达模式在加拿大的移民制度中被广泛使用。它使新斯科舍省能够根据劳动力、技能和移民部(LSI, department of Labour, Skills and Immigration)的自由裁量权,识别和邀请最符合该省不断发展的经济优先事项的申报。这些优先事项可能包括医疗保健、建筑和技工、STEM、自然资源和制造业等行业,以及更广泛的社区和劳动力需求。请继续访问我们的网站,了解已发布的抽签信息。

意向表达模式还为候选人和雇主设定了明确的期望,同时提供了透明和可预测的体验。

我们的意向表达流程如何运作:NSNP和AIP

• 提交意向表达:申请人(NSNP)和雇主(AIP)将继续提交完整申请。这些申报被视为意向表达,并纳入意向表达库。

• 处理抽签:新斯科舍省将定期进行抽签,以选择意向表达进行处理。可能指导选择的因素包括省份优先事项、剩余配额、意向表达库数量和计划完整性考虑。

• 如果被选中:您将收到我们部门的直接沟通,确认您的申报已被选中进行处理。被选中意味着您的申报符合当前优先事项。这不保证获得批准。

• 如果未被选中:您的申报将保留在意向表达库中。如果您未收到沟通,您的状态没有变化。

现有申报

所有提交给新斯科舍省移民计划的申报——无论何时提交——均被视为意向表达。符合资格标准或拥有案件ID不保证您的申报将被处理。

处理通知

如果您的申报被选中进行处理,我们将与您联系。如果您未收到我们的消息,它仍保留在意向表达库中,没有新的更新。您不需要询问,因为如果您的申报状态变化,我们将通知您。

常见问题

问:这一公告对我现有的申报有何影响?

答:您的申报保留在意向表达库中,无需采取任何行动。如果它被选中进行处理,您将收到我们部门的直接沟通。在此之前,您的申报状态没有变化,我们没有进一步的更新。

问:我的意向表达何时会被选中进行处理?

答:我们无法为意向表达的选择提供时间表。我们无法保证您的意向表达将被选中进行处理。您可能希望监视我们的网站,了解定期抽签信息。

问:这一公告是否影响AIP指定?

答:是的。AIP指定申报被视为意向表达,将遵循上述相同流程。

问:我仍然需要提交完整申请吗?

答:是的。提交完整申请的要求保持不变。请确保您的申报是完整的,以避免处理延迟。

问:我的申报在意向表达库中将停留多长时间?

答:您的申报将在意向表达库中保留,直到被抽中或另行通知。时间表因多个因素而异,包括可用名额、劳动力市场优先事项和意向表达数量。新斯科舍省仅在您的申报被选中时才会与您联系。

问:如果我的申报被选中,这是否意味着它可能会被批准?

答:如果您的申报被选中进行处理,不保证它将被批准。

问:我如何获得我文件的更新?

答:您将收到我们部门的直接沟通,确认您的申报已被选中进行处理。同时,您可以访问我们的网站liveinnovascotia.ca,了解最新的计划信息。

问:您将优先考虑哪些申报?

答:新斯科舍省将优先考虑符合当前经济和劳动力市场需求的申报。优先领域可能包括医疗保健、建筑和技工、STEM、自然资源、制造业和全省其他劳动力需求等行业。这些优先事项将根据劳动力市场数据和政府指导随时间推移而演变。更新的选择信息将在我们的网站上发布。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大新斯科舍省正式推行「意向表达」(EOI)流程,对所有新斯科舍省提名计划(NSNP)项目和大西洋移民计划(AIP)的指定与背书流进行甄选,以应对申请数量远超配额的局面,同时向申请人和雇主提供更清晰、更可预测的流程。这一政策对所有想要移民至该省的候选人和雇主产生直接影响。

items.title: Updates to Rural Renewal Stream
items.bodyEn:
Effective January 1, 2026, the Alberta Advantage Immigration Program (AAIP) will implement changes to the Rural Renewal Stream (RRS). Any applications received on or after this date, will be subject to the new criteria.

Due to the reduced allocation by the Government of Canada, AAIP must strategically manage its nomination spaces to align with Government of Alberta priorities, such as rural development, economic diversification and high-demand occupations.

As interest in the program has grown, challenges have emerged such as endorsement volumes exceeding available nomination spaces under RRS .

The changes include:

• establishment of endorsement allocation limits for each designated community

• introduction of a one-year validity period for the Endorsement of Candidate Letters

• implementation of a TEER – Based Endorsement Model

• changing eligibility for foreign nationals within Canada to require them to have a valid work permit at the time of application and assessment

Detailed information on these updates is available on the RRS eligibility and community designation webpage.
items.bodyZh:
从2026年1月1日起生效，Alberta Advantage Immigration Program (AAIP)阿尔伯塔优势移民项目将对Rural Renewal Stream (RRS)农村更新流进行变更。任何在此日期或之后收到的申请将受新标准约束。

由于加拿大联邦政府减少了配额分配，AAIP必须战略性地管理其提名空间，以符合阿尔伯塔省政府的优先事项，例如农村发展、经济多元化和高需求职业。

随着该项目的关注度增加，出现了一些挑战，例如背书申请量超过RRS可用的提名空间。

这些变更包括：

• 为各指定社区建立背书配额分配限额

• 引入Endorsement of Candidate Letters候选人背书信一年有效期

• 实施基于TEER职业分级制的背书模式

• 改变加拿大境内外国申请人的资格要求，要求其在申请和评估时持有有效工作许可

这些更新的详细信息可在RRS资格认定和社区指定网页上获取。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大阿尔伯塔省农村更新移民项目从2026年1月起调整申请标准,包括限制各社区背书配额、设立背书信有效期、采用职业分级制筛选模式,以及要求在加外国申请人持有有效工作许可。这些变化影响所有该日期后提交的Rural Renewal Stream (RRS)申请人。

items.title: Additional allocations
items.bodyEn:
Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) provided AAIP with an additional 1,528 nominations this year, raising our total allocation for 2025 to 6,403. Details regarding the allocation by stream will be updated soon on our processing page.

This expanded capacity strengthens our ability to support regional labour market needs and advance key provincial priorities.
items.bodyZh:
加拿大移民、难民和公民部(Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada, IRCC)为AAIP(阿尔伯塔省提名项目 Alberta Accelerator and Immigrant Program)额外提供了1,528个提名名额,这一年度配额已提升至6,403个。关于各流(stream)的配额分配详情将很快在我们的处理页面上更新。

这一扩展的容量强化了我们支持地区劳动力市场需求和推进主要省级优先事项的能力。
items.summaryZh: 加拿大移民部(IRCC)追加了1,528个提名名额给AAIP(阿尔伯塔省提名项目),使其2025年总配额达到6,403个,以更好地满足地区劳动力市场需求和支持省级优先事项。

items.title: Nova Scotia Immigration Update – 2025
items.bodyEn:
July 17, 2025 We appreciate the strong interest in Nova Scotia’s economic immigration programs. Please note the following update, as we are receiving more applications than we can approve in 2025.

Who We're Prioritizing in 2025

People already living and working in Nova Scotia with work permits expiring in 2025.

• Of these applicants with work permits expiring in 2025, further prioritization of: Healthcare and social assistance (NAICS 62) Construction (NAICS 23)

• We also look at: Regional needs across the province People who are likely to stay long term in Nova Scotia

Applications from Outside Canada

International applicants in healthcare, social assistance and construction continue to be prioritized.

Most other applications from outside Canada in non-priority sectors will not be processed at this time. Exceptions may be made for employers in the Atlantic Immigration Program hiring in:

• Trucking

• Science and technology

• Clean energy sectors

• Resource Development

Planning for 2026

In 2026, we will prioritize workers in healthcare, social assistance and construction with permits expiring that year. Other priorities will be set once we receive our 2026 allocation from the federal government.

Additional Notes

• Not all eligible applications will be processed

• Meeting program criteria does not guarantee approval

• You must continue to have legal status in Canada

• You must tell us about any changes to your status or job
items.bodyZh:
**2025年新斯科舍省移民更新**

2025年7月17日 我们感谢各界对新斯科舍省经济移民项目的强烈关注。请留意以下更新,因为我们收到的申请数量超过了2025年能够批准的数量。

**2025年优先考虑对象**

已在新斯科舍省生活和工作、工作许可证将于2025年到期的人士。

• 对于这些工作许可证将于2025年到期的申请人,我们进一步优先考虑: 医疗和社会援助(NAICS 62) 建筑(NAICS 23)

• 我们还考虑: 全省的地区需求 可能长期留在新斯科舍省的人士

**来自加拿大以外的申请**

医疗、社会援助和建筑领域的国际申请人继续被优先考虑。

来自加拿大以外的其他非优先行业申请大多数情况下目前不会被处理。以下情况例外,即大西洋移民项目(Atlantic Immigration Program)中的雇主在以下行业招聘:

• 卡车运输

• 科学和技术

• 清洁能源部门

• 资源开发

**2026年规划**

2026年,我们将优先考虑医疗、社会援助和建筑领域中许可证将于该年到期的工作人士。一旦我们从联邦政府收到2026年配额,将制定其他优先事项。

**其他说明**

• 并非所有符合条件的申请都会被处理

• 符合项目标准不保证获批

• 您必须继续在加拿大保持合法身份

• 您必须向我们通报任何身份或工作变化
items.summaryZh: 加拿大新斯科舍省因2025年申请超量,调整移民审批优先级：优先处理持工作许可证即将到期的在省内工作人士,尤其是医疗、社会援助和建筑领域;国际申请人中仅保留上述三个优先行业及大西洋移民项目特定部门(卡车运输、科技、清洁能源、资源开发)的审批,其他行业申请暂不处理。
